content.name: AIAP Creator v1.2.0 — Generator

output_mode.L0: Output JSON in ENGLISH: {intent, confidence, route, state, op}
output_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.summary: Generates AISOP/AISIP/DUAL files with sub_mermaid architecture (scaffold/content/tooling). ThreeDimTest (C1-C7, I1-I13, D1-D10) with MF1-MF38 multi-file audit, FORMAT_DETECT for dual-format parsing, traffic light classification, I-SELF-REFERENTIAL scoring, TOOL-COMPUTED deterministic checks, semantic regression detection, fractal exempt drift verification. ~25K tokens/invocation.
content.description: Generator module with sub_mermaid architecture: generate_scaffold (AIAP.md, agent_card, safety_card), generate_content (AISOP files, evolution, MF audit), generate_tooling (UI, tool dirs). Creates AISOP JSON from plans, runs ThreeDimTest (Cognitive/Intrinsic/Detail), classifies fixes by traffic light, saves drafts. Supports AISOP/AISIP/DUAL format generation, I11 identity verification, MF1-MF38 multi-file audit, prompt template management, LICENSE generation, STANDARD RANGE TRACE. ~25K tokens/invocation.

params.plan: object|null, default: null, Accept from Research Propose. Null if modifying existing.
params.modified_file_path: string|null, default: null, Accept from ModifyFlow for re-test. Single file: file path string. Multi-file (Pattern B+): directory path — Generate will scan for all .aisop.json files within.
params.workspace_dir: string, REQUIRED: Default workspace directory. For single-format modes: AISOP uses [AISOP AIAP Store Directory], AISIP uses [AISIP AIAP Store Directory]. For DUAL mode: serves as fallback if aisop_workspace_dir is null. Both paths are injected in system instruction. User override takes precedence.
params.quality_threshold: string, default: standard, strict=A>=3.6, standard=B>=2.7, relaxed=C>=2.0 (weighted).
params.evolution_plan: object|null, default: null, Accept from EvolveFlow. Contains confirmed LEVEL_A/B/C changes. Null if not evolution.
params.evolution_context: object|null, default: null, Accept from Pipeline. Contains old_version_context for evolution verification. Null if Create mode or Modify-only. Schema per STANDARD.data_contracts.evolution_context.
params.module_hashes: object|null, default: null, Accept from Pipeline PipelineStart. Content hashes of target sub-module files for incremental generation. Keys are module_ids, values are SHA-256 hashes. Null if not Pattern B+ or not Evolve mode.
params.wave_context: object|null|null, default: null, Provided by main.aisop.json Generate1 when wave-based generation is active. Contains { wave_index: number, wave_files: [string], total_waves: number, completed_waves: [{ wave_index, files, quality_score }] }. If null, single-pass generation (backward compatible).
params.aisop_workspace_dir: string|null, default: null, Optional. [AISOP AIAP Store Directory] path for DUAL mode. When target_language='DUAL', .aisop.json files are saved to {aisop_workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. If null, falls back to workspace_dir. Passed from main.aisop.json pipeline_context.aisop_output_dir.
params.aisip_workspace_dir: string|null, default: null, Optional. [AISIP AIAP Store Directory] path for DUAL mode split-directory saving. When target_language='DUAL', .aisip.json files are saved to {aisip_workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. REQUIRED for DUAL mode. Passed from main.aisop.json pipeline_context.aisip_output_dir. Null for single-format modes.

content.system_prompt:
AISOP code generator and quality tester. Produce structurally valid output. Enforce Mermaid-function synchronization rigorously. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.
content.fractal_exempt: 21 functional nodes distributed across 4 sub_mermaid sub-graphs (main 12 + generate_scaffold 2 + generate_content 5 + generate_tooling 2). Sub-graphs execute sequentially: scaffold → content → tooling. Maximum single-path execution 21 nodes (new plan path through all 3 sub-graphs).

aisop.main:
graph TD
    Start[Plan or Modified File] --> RouteGate{Route?}
    RouteGate -- New Plan --> ReadTemplate[Read AISOP Template]
    RouteGate -- Modified File --> ThreeDimTest[Three-Dimensional Test]
    ReadTemplate --> GenerateScaffold[aisop.generate_scaffold]
    GenerateScaffold --> GenerateContent[aisop.generate_content]
    GenerateContent --> GenerateTooling[aisop.generate_tooling]
    GenerateTooling --> ThreeDimTest
    ThreeDimTest --> TrafficClassify[Traffic Light Classification]
    TrafficClassify --> PostGenCheck{Unresolved Red?}
    PostGenCheck -- Yes --> PauseHuman[Pause for Human]
    PostGenCheck -- No --> ApplyFixes[Apply Green+Yellow Fixes]
    PauseHuman -- Approved --> ApplyFixes
    PauseHuman -- Rejected --> SaveDraft[Save Draft with Warnings]
    ApplyFixes --> SaveDraft
    SaveDraft --> ReflectionCheck[Reflection Check]
    ReflectionCheck --> endNode((End))
aisop.generate_scaffold:
graph TD
    ScaffoldStart[Scaffold Input] --> PrepareAIAPMd[Prepare AIAP.md + License + Security]
    PrepareAIAPMd --> GenerateAgentCard[Generate Agent Card + Safety Card]
    GenerateAgentCard --> ScaffoldEnd((End))
aisop.generate_content:
graph TD
    ContentStart[Content Input] --> GenerateAISOP[Generate AISOP Files]
    GenerateAISOP --> EvolutionVerify[Evolution Verification]
    EvolutionVerify --> MermaidSync[Mermaid Sync + Impact Scan]
    MermaidSync --> MFAudit[Multi-File Audit]
    MFAudit --> FractalCheck[Fractal + Pattern Check]
    FractalCheck --> ContentEnd((End))
aisop.generate_tooling:
graph TD
    ToolingStart[Tooling Input] --> GenerateUI[UI Generation]
    GenerateUI --> GenerateToolDirs[Tool Directory Generation]
    GenerateToolDirs --> ToolingEnd((End))

Start.step1: Receive plan or modified_file_path. Pass to RouteGate.
Start.constraints: (A) Type guard — string/object only. (B) Size guard — plan >500KB -> reject. (C) Injection guard — sanitize paths. (D) Path guard — reject any path not resolving within workspace_dir (including absolute paths outside workspace and '..' traversal). (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize.
Start.Error: Invalid input -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: request re-supply -> inform.

RouteGate.step1: Route: plan non-null -> ReadTemplate. modified_file_path non-null -> ThreeDimTest.
RouteGate.Error: Ambiguous input -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: default ReadTemplate route -> inform.

ReadTemplate.step1: TEMPLATE DISCOVERY: Load AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json from pipeline_context.creator_directory (set by main.aisop.json PipelineStart). DO NOT scan workspace_dir for STANDARD files — Creator's own directory is the authoritative source. If pipeline_context.creator_directory is not set (standalone mode), fallback: locate directory containing this generate.aisop.json file. Read AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json. Extract quality_checklist and extension_registry. EXTENSION LOADING (per core extension_registry.loading_protocol): (1) ALWAYS load AIAP_Standard.security.aisop.json — security checks mandatory, (2) load AIAP_Standard.ecosystem.aisop.json if target Pattern >= B, (3) load AIAP_Standard.performance.aisop.json if in pipeline context or quality_threshold='strict'. Merge extension quality_checklist items into core checklist. Verify all loaded extensions have matching template_version.
ReadTemplate.step2: Read AIAP_Protocol.md from same directory. Apply Pattern selection: 1 function -> A, 2+ -> B, 2+ with shared logic -> C, sub-modules -> D. For Pattern G programs with aiap_tools/: load I13 sub-checks (g)(h)(i)(j) and MF16 sub-checks (e)(f)(g)(h) from STANDARD.core and STANDARD.ecosystem for aiap_tools validation.
ReadTemplate.step3: Extract complexity + archetype from plan. Finalize generation strategy.
ReadTemplate.step4: PROMPT TEMPLATE MANAGEMENT: read prompt_templates.json from aisop_creator directory (same location as AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json). If found, load domain-appropriate templates: system_prompt templates, Error field format templates, boundary rule templates, constraints templates. Apply loaded templates during generation for consistency. If prompt_templates.json not found, use built-in defaults — this is expected for most installations. Template format: { templates: { system_prompt: string, error_field: string, boundary_rule: string, constraints: string }, domain_overrides: { [domain]: { ...partial template overrides } } }.
ReadTemplate.constraints: Template not found -> use AI knowledge as fallback. Discovery priority: (1) same directory as generate.aisop.json, (2) scan workspace_dir subdirectories, (3) AI knowledge. (D) Path guard — validate discovered paths within workspace_dir, reject '..'.
ReadTemplate.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use built-in STANDARD knowledge with 'unverified template' note -> inform.

GenerateScaffold.step1: DELEGATE to aisop.generate_scaffold. Pass plan, template data, and evolution_plan to scaffold sub-graph for metadata and structural file generation.
GenerateScaffold.constraints: Sequential dependency: scaffold output (AIAP.md, agent_card.json) is consumed by generate_content.
GenerateScaffold.Error: Scaffold fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip scaffold, proceed with content generation using plan data directly -> inform.

GenerateContent.step1: DELEGATE to aisop.generate_content. Pass scaffold output (AIAP.md metadata, agent_card data) plus plan, template, evolution_plan, module_hashes, wave_context to content sub-graph for core AISOP file generation.
GenerateContent.constraints: Sequential dependency: content output (.aisop.json files) is consumed by generate_tooling.
GenerateContent.Error: Content generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: minimal scaffold with TODO markers -> inform.

GenerateTooling.step1: DELEGATE to aisop.generate_tooling. Pass content output (generated .aisop.json files, AIAP.md) to tooling sub-graph for UI and tool directory generation.
GenerateTooling.constraints: Final generation stage. Output feeds into ThreeDimTest.
GenerateTooling.Error: Tooling generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip tooling (UI and tool_dirs are optional), proceed to ThreeDimTest -> inform.

ScaffoldStart.step1: Receive plan, template data (quality_checklist, extension_registry, prompt_templates), and evolution_plan. Prepare generation context for scaffold files.
ScaffoldStart.constraints: (A) Type guard — validate plan/template structure. (B) Evolution mode: if evolution_plan provided, load existing AIAP.md for incremental update.
ScaffoldStart.Error: Invalid input -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: request re-supply -> inform.

PrepareAIAPMd.step1: AIAP.md GENERATION: for new creations, generate AIAP.md with: all 14 required frontmatter fields (6 governance + 8 project), governance statement body section, feature overview body section, usage instructions body section, AIAP closing seal. Status set to 'draft' for new creations. FLOW_FORMAT FIELD (v1.14.0): ALWAYS include flow_format in Project Fields. Derive from pipeline_context.target_language: 'AISOP' (default, Mermaid flow), 'AISIP' (JSON flow dict). If target_language is not set or undefined, default to 'AISOP'. This field is REQUIRED per §3.1 — declares which serialization format the program's §4 flow graph uses. DUAL MODE SPLIT: when target_language='DUAL', generate TWO variants of AIAP.md — one with flow_format='AISOP' (written to aisop_workspace_dir) and one with flow_format='AISIP' (written to aisip_workspace_dir). Each directory's AIAP.md is self-describing for the files it contains. All other AIAP.md fields remain identical between the two copies.
PrepareAIAPMd.step2: LICENSE & AUTHORSHIP FIELDS: (A) LICENSE (§3.1 mandatory): prompt user for SPDX license identifier (per STANDARD.license_spec); default 'proprietary' if not specified. If license='proprietary', require terms_url or contact (at least one). Common choices: MIT (permissive), Apache-2.0 (patent protection), GPL-3.0-only (copyleft), CC-BY-4.0 (documentation), proprietary (commercial). For evolutions, preserve existing license field unless user explicitly requests change. (B) AUTHOR (§3.2 optional): prompt user for author identifier. If user skips, write field with empty value: 'author: ""'. Do NOT omit the field — empty placeholder ensures discoverability. (C) COPYRIGHT (§3.2 optional): prompt user for copyright notice. If user skips, write field with empty value: 'copyright: ""'. Do NOT omit the field — empty placeholder ensures discoverability. For evolutions, preserve existing author/copyright fields unless user explicitly requests change.
PrepareAIAPMd.step3: FRONTMATTER EXTENSION (P1): ensure AIAP.md frontmatter includes extended fields per STANDARD.core agent_card_spec.extended_frontmatter_fields — skills[], capabilities, authentication. Preserve existing frontmatter fields. Add missing fields with sensible defaults (skills=[] if no NLU, capabilities=all-false if not declared).
PrepareAIAPMd.step4: SECURITY SCHEMES (P4a): when generating AIAP.md, include securitySchemes based on trust_level per STANDARD.core security_authentication_spec.trust_mapping: T1 -> no securitySchemes section, T2 -> { "bearer": { "type": "http", "scheme": "bearer" } }, T3 -> { "oauth2": { "type": "oauth2", "flows": { "authorizationCode": { "authorizationUrl": "...", "tokenUrl": "..." } } } }, T4 -> { "oauth2": { ... }, "mutualTls": { "type": "mutualTls" } }. AIAP.md explicit declaration overrides auto-suggestion. GOVERNANCE_HASH PLACEHOLDER (CR9 v1.2.0, Create mode only): When generating AIAP.md in Create mode, write governance_hash field with placeholder value: 'governance_hash: "pending — computed in ReviewStep"'. This ensures the frontmatter field exists for ThreeDimTest D6 to detect, while signaling that the actual hash is deferred to ReviewStep (which has all final .aisop.json content). SKIP: for Evolve mode (AIAP.md already has governance_hash from prior version). AISIP FORMAT AWARENESS (B3 v1.9.0, updated B2 v1.14.0 — output_format→flow_format unification): When pipeline_context.target_language IN ('AISIP', 'DUAL'), add format metadata to AIAP.md frontmatter: (a) flow_format field (§3.1 REQUIRED): set to 'AISOP' or 'AISIP' per directory (see PrepareAIAPMd.step1 DUAL MODE SPLIT). NOTE: the legacy 'output_format' AIAP.md field is DEPRECATED in favor of 'flow_format' — do NOT generate 'output_format' in AIAP.md frontmatter. The internal routing variable output_format (used in step9 FORMAT ROUTING) remains as an INTERNAL pipeline variable, distinct from the AIAP.md field. (b) For AISIP programs: update summary to include 'AISIP V1.0.0 (type-free JSON flow)' descriptor. (c) For DUAL programs (B1 v1.11.0): update summary to include 'DUAL (AISOP Mermaid + AISIP JSON flow)' descriptor. Add AIAP.md fields: supported_formats=['aisop', 'aisip'], dual_language=true. DUAL governance_hash: each directory computes its own hash from its own files (see B3 v1.14.0). (d) agent_card.json skills enhancement: when pipeline output_format='aisip' or 'dual', add 'aisip_generation' to capabilities.offered list. SEED↔FLOW_FORMAT SYNC (B4 v1.14.0): when generating AIAP.md, ensure seed domain aligns with flow_format — flow_format='AISOP' → seed='aisop.dev', flow_format='AISIP' → seed='aisip.dev'. DUAL mode: aisop directory seed='aisop.dev', aisip directory seed='aisip.dev'. BACKWARD COMPATIBLE: when target_language='AISOP' or undefined, flow_format='AISOP' and seed='aisop.dev' (preserve existing). SKIP: when target_language is not set (legacy pipelines).
PrepareAIAPMd.constraints: AIAP.md content is PREPARED but not written — passed to ReviewStep for single-ownership write (per main.aisop.json ReviewStep.step3). License defaults to 'proprietary' if not specified.
PrepareAIAPMd.Error: AIAP.md preparation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: minimal AIAP.md with required fields only -> inform.

GenerateAgentCard.step1: AGENT CARD GENERATION (P1/P11): after generating all .aisop.json files and AIAP.md, ALWAYS generate agent_card.json per STANDARD.core agent_card_spec.agent_card_derivation rules — name from AIAP.md name, description from AIAP.md summary (first sentence or first 200 chars), version from AIAP.md version, provider from AIAP.md author + identity, pattern from AIAP.md pattern, tags from AIAP.md tags[], skills[] from main NLU intents with inputModes/outputModes inferred from tools[], capabilities from capabilities_spec (default all-false), trust_level from AIAP.md trust_level.level, securitySchemes mapped by trust_level (T1→none, T2→bearer, T3→oauth2, T4→oauth2+mutualTls), tools from AIAP.md tools[].name, modules from AIAP.md modules[].id, status from AIAP.md status. Add _generated metadata block with generator identity and timestamp. WRITE agent_card.json to {workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/agent_card.json (root level, alongside AIAP.md). This is UNCONDITIONAL — all programs regardless of Pattern get an agent_card.json. Atomic write: .tmp then rename. For evolutions: regenerate agent_card.json from updated AIAP.md to keep in sync.
GenerateAgentCard.step2: SAFETY CARD GENERATION (P14): Within agent_card.json, generate safety_card object: (1) risk_level — derive from trust_level (T1→minimal, T2→limited, T3→high, T4→high), elevate if crisis/medical/financial intents present. (2) data_handling — extract from memory module: storage_type, retention_policy, encryption. If no memory module: {storage:'none', retention:'session', encryption:'n/a'}. (3) limitations — extract from description, known constraints, max token budget. (4) compliance_markers — populated from Research3 compliance_notes if available. UNCONDITIONAL: all programs generate safety_card regardless of Pattern.
GenerateAgentCard.constraints: UNCONDITIONAL: all programs generate agent_card.json and safety_card regardless of Pattern. Atomic write: .tmp then rename.
GenerateAgentCard.Error: Agent card generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: minimal agent_card.json with name+version only -> inform.

ContentStart.step1: Receive scaffold output (AIAP.md metadata, agent_card data) plus plan, template, evolution_plan, module_hashes, wave_context. Prepare generation context for AISOP file generation.
ContentStart.constraints: (A) Verify scaffold output contains required AIAP.md metadata. (B) If scaffold was skipped (fallback), derive metadata from plan directly.
ContentStart.Error: Invalid scaffold data -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use plan metadata as substitute -> inform.

GenerateAISOP.step1: NODE BUDGET ADVISORY (CR7 v1.2.0, Create mode only): Before generating AISOP files, compute recommended node budget based on Pattern and module count. Rules: Pattern A = 1 module, 6-12 nodes. Pattern B = 2-3 modules, 6-14 nodes each. Pattern D/F = 3-8 modules, 6-14 nodes each. Pattern D+G = 5-12 modules, 6-14 nodes each. Total budget = modules × 10 (average). Per-module budget: 6-14 (C5 threshold 16, leave margin). If scope_recommendation from Research1 exists, compare against budget: any module with estimated_nodes > 14 → YELLOW advisory 'Module {name} estimated at {N} nodes, exceeding 14-node recommended maximum. Consider splitting or reducing scope.' Any module with estimated_nodes > 16 → RED advisory 'Module {name} estimated at {N} nodes, will trigger C5 violation. Must split or reduce scope.' Log: 'NODE BUDGET: {total_budget} total, {module_count} modules, per-module range 6-14.' SKIP: for Evolve mode (existing programs have established node counts). TOOL ANNOTATIONS: for each tool declared in the plan, generate annotations object with read_only, destructive, idempotent, open_world flags based on tool behavior. Defaults: all false. Overrides: file_system with write → read_only=false; google_search/web_browser → open_world=true, read_only=true, idempotent=true; tools with delete operations → destructive=true. JSON SCHEMA GENERATION: for each new module, auto-generate json_schema (JSON Schema Draft 2020-12) describing the module's output structure. Derive from: (a) declared parameters with type annotations, (b) output fields in function steps, (c) STANDARD data_contracts if output maps to known contract. Generate AISOP: [system.content] + [user.content.aisop.main Mermaid] + [user.content.functions]. Apply fractal pattern (A-D). NULL HANDLING: if downstream modules will receive research_context=null (e.g., standalone Generate without Pipeline), ensure generated AISOP's Modify-consuming functions include null-safe fallback logic.
GenerateAISOP.step2: MULTI-FILE: if plan.multi_file=true, identify affected sub-modules and generate changes per file. COORDINATION ORDER: sub-modules first (bottom-up), then main routing file last. Ensure cross-file data contracts (parameter names, output formats) remain consistent. INCREMENTAL GENERATION: if module_hashes provided, compare each sub-module's current hash against evolution_plan changes. Sub-modules with no planned changes AND unchanged hash -> skip regeneration, preserve existing file. Only regenerate sub-modules that: (a) have planned LEVEL_A/B/C changes targeting them, OR (b) depend on a changed module's output (MF4 cross-file contract affected). INCREMENTAL SKIP CONDITIONS (all must be true): (1) module_hash unchanged from previous version, (2) AIAP_Standard template version unchanged (standard_version_hash matches), (3) quality_threshold configuration unchanged, (4) no cross-module dependency changes detected (no upstream module in dependency_matrix was regenerated). If ANY condition is false, the module MUST be regenerated. Track skip decisions in pipeline_metadata.incremental_decisions: [{module, skipped: bool, reason: string}]. PATTERN E MEMORY DEPENDENCY PROPAGATION: when target program uses Pattern E, apply additional skip-prevention rule: if memory.aisop.json (or any module writing to memory/ directory) has changed, automatically mark all modules that READ from memory/ paths as 'dependency changed' — do NOT skip their regeneration even if their own module_hash is unchanged. This prevents stale data contract assumptions when shared state changes. Implementation: scan each module's functions for file_system read operations with paths containing 'memory/', cross-reference with changed modules' write paths. CACHE SAFETY: if quality_threshold='strict', disable incremental skip and force full regeneration for maximum correctness assurance.
GenerateAISOP.step3: METADATA-ONLY MODE: when evolution_plan.metadata_only_mode=true (all accepted changes target AIAP.md only, no .aisop.json functional changes), activate metadata-only field updates for .aisop.json files: (a) for each .aisop.json file, update ONLY metadata fields — system.content.name (version string), system.content.version, and system.content.description (version attribution) — without regenerating functional content (Mermaid graph, functions, steps, constraints, Error fields), (b) preserve all existing functional content byte-for-byte, (c) record in generation_report: { metadata_only_mode: true, files_metadata_updated: [string], functional_content_preserved: true }. GUARD: if any evolution_plan change targets functional content (new steps, new constraints, new Mermaid nodes, new functions), disable metadata_only_mode and proceed with normal generation. FALLBACK DETECTION: if metadata_only_mode is not explicitly set but evolution_plan contains ONLY changes with affected_modules=['AIAP_MD'], auto-activate metadata_only_mode with advisory log. This avoids unnecessary full regeneration risk when only governance metadata changes.
GenerateAISOP.step4: WAVE-BASED GENERATION (P2b): if wave_context is provided, generate ONLY the files listed in wave_context.wave_files. Use completed_waves results for forward-declaration stubs of cross-file references. Maintain wave isolation — each wave's output must be independently valid JSON. INTER-WAVE QUALITY CHECK: between waves, run quick ThreeDimTest on completed wave files. SCOPE: C (Cognitive) and D (Detail) dimensions only — skip I (Intrinsic) dimension to reduce token cost. TIER: retain the target program's actual tier classification (do NOT downgrade to Simple tier). This ensures cognitive and detail checks are accurate for the target complexity level while saving the Intrinsic dimension for the full post-generation ThreeDimTest. If wave_context is null, use single-pass generation (backward compatible).
GenerateAISOP.step5: OPERATION VOCABULARY (P2c): PREFER standard verbs from STANDARD.core operation_vocabulary (RUN, DELEGATE, ROUTE, GATE, COLLECT, EMIT, CLASSIFY) at the beginning of step descriptions in generated .aisop.json files. Standard verbs are RECOMMENDED for semantic clarity but not REQUIRED — free-text descriptions remain valid. Example: 'DELEGATE to research.aisop.json for domain analysis' instead of 'Call the research module'. MULTIMODAL INFERENCE (P5): when generating programs that use multimodal tools, apply STANDARD.core multimodal_message.mode_inference_rules to set appropriate inputModes/outputModes in AIAP.md skills[]. For each tool in tools[], infer supported modes: image_generation -> 'image/png', tts -> 'audio/wav', speech_recognition -> 'audio/*', code_execution -> 'application/json'. Default to 'text/plain' for unmatched tools.
GenerateAISOP.step6: A2A BRIDGE CONFIG GENERATION: for Pattern F/G programs with trust_level >= T3, auto-generate A2A bridge configuration per STANDARD.ecosystem.a2a_bridge — derive bridge_config from program capabilities: bridge_enabled=true, supported_interfaces from capabilities_spec (default ['message/send', 'message/stream']), auth_scheme from security_schemes, timeout_seconds from task_lifecycle defaults. Delegate detailed config generation to advisor A2AAdvisor step4. For non-eligible programs (Pattern A-E without explicit opt-in, or trust_level < T3), skip with note 'A2A bridge not applicable for this program configuration'. NOTE: A2A bridge config is separate from agent_card.json which is generated unconditionally in AGENT CARD GENERATION above. Bridge config is protocol-level interop, Agent Card is program-level self-description.
GenerateAISOP.step7: EXTENSION RECOMMENDATION: analyze program pattern, tools, and capabilities to recommend applicable URI-based extensions per STANDARD.extension_registry.remote_extensions — (a) Pattern E programs → recommend memory-tier extension, (b) programs with safety modules → recommend safety-screening extension, (c) programs with NLU routing and 5+ intents → recommend intent-vectors extension. Present recommendations as advisory list in generation_report. Do not auto-activate — user must declare extensions in AIAP.md frontmatter.
GenerateAISOP.step8: LOADING_MODE INJECTION: For every generated .aisop.json file, inject 'loading_mode': 'normal' into system.content. This is a REQUIRED protocol field per AIAP_Protocol §2.5. Default 'normal' (all functions loaded at once). Alternative 'node' (on-demand loading by runtime) is only set when user explicitly requests it. Verify: every generated .aisop.json contains loading_mode field — missing → auto-inject with 'normal' and log 'LOADING_MODE: auto-injected normal for {file}'.
GenerateAISOP.step9: AISIP TYPE-FREE GENERATION (B2 v1.9.0 — corrected per 15 phased evolution plan, E1/E2/E5 error fixes): Read pipeline_context.target_language (set by PipelineStart.step_language). FORMAT ROUTING (output_format is an INTERNAL pipeline routing variable — NOT an AIAP.md field; the AIAP.md field is flow_format per §3.1): (a) target_language='AISOP' or undefined → output_format='aisop' (default, backward compatible), (b) target_language='AISIP' → output_format='aisip', (c) target_language='DUAL' → output_format='dual' — generate BOTH .aisop.json and .aisip.json simultaneously (B1 v1.11.0 Phase 3). AISIP GENERATION (when output_format='aisip'): Generate .aisip.json files with TYPE-FREE nodes per AISIP V1.0.0 protocol. (1) SYSTEM METADATA: generate { protocol: 'AISIP V1.0.0', id: '{program_name}.main', name: '{PascalCase}', version, summary, description, tools, system_prompt } — identical structure to AISOP except protocol field. (2) AISIP FLOW GRAPH (aisip.main) — TYPE-FREE: translate control flow design to JSON flow dict. *** NEVER ADD 'type' FIELD TO ANY NODE ***. For each designed node: sequential → {"next": ["target"]}, conditional → {"branches": {"cond1": "target1", "cond2": "target2"}}, terminal → {}, error path → {"next": ["target"], "error": "error_handler"}, parallel fork → {"next": ["branch1", "branch2"]}, join → {"wait_for": ["branch1", "branch2"], "next": ["target"]}, sub-task (node-level) → {"delegate_to": "sub_name", "next": ["return"]}. ORDERING: first key = entry node, last key = {} (end). FLOW LAYER KEYS: ONLY topology fields allowed in flow nodes: next, branches, error, wait_for, delegate_to. NO extension fields in flow layer — the 7 RESERVED_KEYS (on_error, retry_policy, context_filter, output_mapping, map, join, constraints — per AIAP_Standard.runtime_extensions.aisop.json) belong EXCLUSIVELY in functions layer. (3) FUNCTIONS — 100% IDENTICAL TO AISOP: functions contain steps (step1, step2, ...) plus 7 RESERVED_KEYS (on_error, retry_policy, context_filter, output_mapping, map, join, constraints). When target_language='DUAL' (B1 v1.11.0): functions are directly reused between AISOP and AISIP versions — same dict, zero duplication. DUAL GENERATION PROCEDURE: (a) DESIGN functions once (shared), (b) GENERATE .aisop.json with Mermaid flow + shared functions, (c) GENERATE .aisip.json with JSON flow dict (topology-only: next/branches/error/wait_for/delegate_to) + shared functions (byte-identical copy), (d) VERIFY diff(aisop.functions, aisip.functions) == empty. If functions diverge → RED 'DUAL-FUNCTIONS-MISMATCH'. (e) SPLIT-DIRECTORY SAVE (B1 v1.12.0): .aisop.json files → {aisop_workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/, .aisip.json files → {aisip_workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. Both directories share the same {name}_aiap/ subdirectory name. AIAP.md is written to BOTH directories with one difference: flow_format='AISOP' in aisop_workspace_dir, flow_format='AISIP' in aisip_workspace_dir (all other fields identical). quality_baseline.json and agent_card.json are written identically to BOTH directories for independent program completeness. Log: 'DUAL: generated {aisop_count} .aisop.json → {aisop_workspace_dir}, {aisip_count} .aisip.json → {aisip_workspace_dir}, functions_reused=true'. (4) TOPOLOGY vs BEHAVIOR DISTINCTION: flow layer 'error': 'ErrorHandler' = topology (draws dotted line). functions layer 'on_error': {...} = behavior (runtime routing). Both are valid and complementary. (5) DELEGATE TWO-LEVEL SUPPORT (E5 fix): node-level delegate: flow node {"delegate_to": "sub_name"} for sub-flow invocation. step-level delegate: function step 'RUN module.function' for cross-module calls. Both supported, not conflicting. (6) instruction field: 'ASSERT RUN aisip.main'. (7) VERIFICATION: after generating .aisip.json, auto-verify: (a) no node contains 'type' key, (b) no flow node contains RESERVED_KEYS, (c) all branches/next/error/wait_for/delegate_to references resolve to existing nodes, (d) at least one node is {} (end), (e) first key is entry node. Log: 'FORMAT: output_format={format}, files_generated={count}, type_free_verified={bool}'. SKIP: when output_format='aisop' (no changes to existing AISOP generation logic).
GenerateAISOP.step9_5: AISIP CONTENT VALIDATION (B2 v1.9.0): CONDITION: output_format IN ('aisip', 'dual'). (1) VERIFY system metadata completeness: protocol='AISIP V1.0.0', id, name, version MUST be present (§2 required fields). (2) VERIFY instruction field exists and equals 'ASSERT RUN aisip.main'. (3) VERIFY aisip.main entry: first key is entry node (not {} end node). (4) VERIFY end node existence: at least one node value is {} (empty dict = end). (5) VERIFY reference integrity: ALL node names referenced in next[], branches values, error, wait_for[], delegate_to MUST exist as keys in aisip.main. Dangling reference → RED error. (6) VERIFY type-free compliance: FOR each node_name, node_value in aisip.main: IF 'type' in node_value.keys() → RED 'AISIP-TYPE-VIOLATION: node {node_name} contains prohibited type field. Remove type — node type is inferred from structure per AISIP V1.0.0.' (7) VERIFY extension layer compliance: FOR each node_name, node_value in aisip.main: FOR each key in node_value.keys(): IF key in {on_error, retry_policy, context_filter, output_mapping, map, join, constraints} (the 7 RESERVED_KEYS per AIAP_Standard.runtime_extensions.aisop.json) → RED 'AISIP-EXTENSION-VIOLATION: node {node_name} contains extension key {key} in flow layer. Move to functions[{node_name}].' (8) VERIFY flow-key allowlist: allowed_flow_keys = {next, branches, error, wait_for, delegate_to}. FOR each node: unknown_keys = node.keys() - allowed_flow_keys. IF unknown_keys AND node != {} → YELLOW 'AISIP-UNKNOWN-FLOW-KEY: node {node_name} contains {unknown_keys}. Verify intent.' (9) DUAL CONSISTENCY (B1 v1.11.0 — Phase 3 simplified DUAL; functions 100% reuse per corrected phased evolution plan §6.2): When output_format='dual', execute DUAL-C1 verification: (a) EXTRACT functions dicts from both generated files: aisop_funcs = aisop_file.functions, aisip_funcs = aisip_file.functions. (b) DEEP COMPARE: iterate all keys and values recursively. diff_items = deep_diff(aisop_funcs, aisip_funcs). (c) IF diff_items is empty → GREEN 'DUAL-C1: functions 100% identical across formats'. (d) IF diff_items is non-empty → RED 'DUAL-C1-VIOLATION: functions differ between AISOP and AISIP versions. Differences: {diff_items}. AUTO-ALIGN: copy aisop.functions to aisip.functions (AISOP is authoritative source).' (e) NOTE: no need for extract() separation — both formats use identical functions structure. This is the core simplification from corrected phased evolution plan (E3 fix): DUAL reduces to pure topology conversion. SKIP: when output_format IN ('aisop', 'aisip') — single-format mode needs no cross-format consistency check.
GenerateAISOP.step10: Report skipped modules in generation_report: { skipped: [module_id], regenerated: [module_id], reason: { module_id: string } }.
GenerateAISOP.constraints: Apply quality_checklist from STANDARD template. Evolution items dropped -> flag generation_warning. MF4 NEW CONTRACTS: when evolution adds cross-module references, verify the referenced module's output format supports the new reference. COMPLEXITY THRESHOLD ENFORCEMENT: When generating or modifying a node, check stated complexity thresholds within that node. If exceeded: (1) If DECOMPOSITION PLAN exists, accept with WARN. (2) If none, add one. CREATE MODE DATA CONTRACT (INS-008): When generating a multi-module program in CREATE mode, for each pair of communicating modules (A produces data consumed by B), auto-generate a formal data contract in both modules' constraints: { contract_id: '{A}_to_{B}_{data_name}', producer: A, consumer: B, schema: { field_name: type, ... }, required_fields: [...], validation: 'Consumer MUST validate schema on receipt' }. This prevents implicit data contracts that only surface as runtime errors.
GenerateAISOP.Error: Generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: minimal scaffold with TODO markers -> inform.

EvolutionVerify.step1: If evolution_plan provided, implement confirmed changes: LEVEL_A changes (user-confirmed structural changes — Pattern upgrade, module add/remove, tool changes). LEVEL_B changes (user-confirmed or engine-recommended functional changes). LEVEL_C changes (auto-applied incremental improvements). EVOLUTION VERIFICATION: if evolution_context provided, cross-check each implemented change against evolution_context.old_version to verify the change actually modifies the intended target (e.g., if LEVEL_B says 'add DependencyCheck node', verify the node was actually added and connected correctly). Flag any evolution_plan item that could not be verified as implemented. ROUTING PARAMETER AUTO-CREATION: when LEVEL_A adds a new sub-module, check existing sub-modules for routing parameter patterns. If existing sub-modules receive explicit routing parameters from main (e.g., record receives operation_type, report receives report_type), create an equivalent explicit routing parameter for the new sub-module (e.g., analysis_type for a new analysis module). Add the parameter to: (1) the new sub-module's parameters section, (2) the main router's delegation node step that passes parameters, (3) the new sub-module's ModeGate/routing function. If no existing routing parameter pattern exists (all sub-modules use user_request inference), skip this step. NLU CONFUSION MATRIX CHECK: When evolving a main.aisop.json module that contains NLU intent classification: (1) For each new intent added, verify at least one confusion pair exists with each existing intent that could plausibly be confused. (2) Generate a coverage matrix showing which intent-intent pairs have confusion rules. (3) If a new intent has 0 confusion pairs, emit WARN: 'New intent {name} has no confusion pairs — classify as intentional (unique intent) or add pairs for disambiguation.' Minimum: each new intent should have at least 2 confusion pairs with existing intents. TIER-APPROPRIATE FORM: Simple/Standard AISOP -> prefer adding new steps within existing functions. Professional AISOP -> may add new nodes/routes/functions. Evolution items must NOT be silently dropped. FIX REGRESSION PROTECTION: when rewriting an existing file during evolution, before overwriting any constraints, Error, or description text, compare new text against old file's text. If the old text contains a more precise or qualified version of a phrase that the new text generalizes (e.g., old: 'Read-only data loading (budget writes handled by WriteBudget)' vs new: 'Read-only'), PRESERVE the old precise wording. Detection: for each constraints/Error field being rewritten, diff old vs new — if old text contains additional qualifiers, scoping phrases, or cross-references that new text omits, flag as potential regression and preserve old wording. This prevents evolution from undoing fixes applied in prior versions.
EvolutionVerify.step2: DEGRADED ITEM DETECTION (per STANDARD.evolution_rules.cascade_on_reject): If evolution_plan contains items marked as { degraded: true }: (1) For each degraded LEVEL_B/C item: identify the missing_dependency (rejected LEVEL_A item) and the fallback_source. (2) Scan the generated .aisop.json content for references to the missing configuration (e.g., parameter names, config keys from the rejected item). (3) If dangling reference found (generated code references a parameter/config that was part of the rejected LEVEL_A item): flag as DEGRADED_REFERENCE_ERROR — 'Function {function_name}.{step} references {config_key} from rejected LEVEL_A item {item_id}. Auto-substituting with {fallback_source} default value: {default_value}.' (4) Auto-replace the dangling reference with the appropriate default value from STANDARD or hardcoded defaults. (5) Record degraded_items_resolved in generation_report: [{ item_id, function, step, config_key, default_value_used }]. (6) If no degraded items in evolution_plan: skip this check entirely. PERSONALITY ANCHOR PROTECTION (per STANDARD.I_PERSONA + Doc 20 P2.3): When evolving a program that has a system_prompt field in system.content: (1) IDENTIFY PERSONALITY SEGMENTS: scan system_prompt for personality-bearing sentences — those containing identity verbs ('am', 'are', 'engage in'), communication style directives ('Mirror', 'natural dialogue', 'warmth'), transparency declarations ('transparent about being an AI'), and alignment statements ('Align: Human Sovereignty'). Mark these as personality_anchor segments. (2) APPEND-ONLY RULE: new capability descriptions from evolution (e.g., 'Supports Bayesian strategy learning') MUST be appended AFTER existing personality_anchor segments, not interspersed or substituted. (3) DIFF VERIFICATION: after generating evolved system_prompt, compute character-level diff against pre-evolution system_prompt. If any personality_anchor segment was MODIFIED (not just appended to): flag as PERSONALITY_DRIFT_WARNING: 'system_prompt personality segment modified during evolution. Original: "{old_text}". New: "{new_text}". This may alter the program perceived personality.' (4) If PERSONALITY_DRIFT_WARNING triggered: present side-by-side diff to user, require explicit confirmation. Without confirmation, revert to original personality segments + appended new content. (5) Record personality_anchor_check in generation_report: { segments_identified: N, segments_preserved: N, drift_detected: boolean, user_confirmed: boolean }. SKIP: if program has no system_prompt field, skip entirely. EMOTIONAL STATE GATE RECOMMENDATION (B5 v1.18.0): In Evolve mode, for each new step added by LEVEL_B changes that produces user-facing output (modifies response_text, appends prompts, inserts questions, generates recommendations): scan step text for emotional state checking references (mood_trajectory, current_mood, emotional_state, valence, sentiment). If the step does NOT reference any emotional state as a gate condition: flag advisory YELLOW 'B5-EMOTION-GATE: user-facing intervention at {step_id} in {function} has no emotional state gate — consider adding mood check to prevent inappropriate timing when user is distressed.' This is advisory (YELLOW), not blocking (RED) — some interventions (crisis resources, safety alerts) should fire regardless of emotional state. Log: 'EMOTION GATE SCAN: {N} user-facing steps checked, {M} missing emotional gate, {K} exempt (safety/crisis).' SKIP: for non-user-facing programs or Create mode. PRACTICE MODE SIGNAL ISOLATION ADVISORY (C2 v1.18.0): In Evolve mode, when detecting a new 'practice mode', 'role-play mode', 'simulation mode', or 'debate mode' feature (scan new step text for keywords: practice, role-play, roleplay, simulate, debate, interview, rehearse): check if the profiler/signal extraction module has a corresponding isolation flag (e.g., 'if skill_practice_active=true, suppress signal extraction'). If no isolation found and profiler module exists: flag YELLOW 'C2-PRACTICE-ISOLATION: practice/role-play mode at {step_id} may contaminate user profile signals — consider adding practice_mode isolation to profiler signal extraction.' SKIP: if target program has no profiler module or no practice-mode features detected. ALGORITHM SPECIFICATION GUARD (B4 v1.18.0 — Doc 32 GEN-1): When generating or verifying step text, scan for vague algorithm references using patterns: (a) 'similarity > {N}' or 'similarity >= {N}' without specifying algorithm name (cosine, Jaccard, token overlap, edit distance), (b) 'score'/'confidence'/'threshold' with numeric comparison but no computation method defined, (c) 'rank by relevance' without specifying ranking dimensions and weights, (d) 'classify'/'categorize' with threshold but no feature definition. For each detected vague reference: YELLOW 'GEN1-ALGO-VAGUE: {step_id} in {function} uses "{matched_pattern}" without specifying algorithm. Minimum specification: input type, computation method name, output range.' EXEMPTION: references to named standard algorithms (SM-2, TF-IDF, BM25, EWA, Thompson Sampling, Bayesian, SHA-256) are self-documenting and exempt. EXEMPTION: references within constraints that define the algorithm inline (e.g., 'normalized token overlap ratio >= 0.7 where overlap = |tokens_A ∩ tokens_B| / max(|tokens_A|, |tokens_B|)') are exempt — the definition IS the specification. FREQUENCY TRACKING COMPLETENESS (B5 v1.18.0 — Doc 32 GEN-2): When step text contains frequency control patterns ('max {N} per {unit}', 'cooldown', 'rate limit', 'at most {N}', 'no more than {N} per', 'track {var}_count'), verify three completeness conditions: (1) STORAGE — the tracking variable (e.g., frameworks_taught_count, interventions_this_session) has a defined storage location in working_context, profile, or parameters schema. Scan for '{var}' in parameter definitions and working_context schema references. (2) INCREMENT — somewhere in the pipeline, a step explicitly increments the tracking variable ('increment {var}', '{var} += 1', '{var}++', 'update {var}'). (3) RESET — a reset condition exists ('reset at session start', 'decay after {N} sessions', 'clear on new session', '{var} = 0 when'). RESULT: any of the three missing → YELLOW 'GEN2-FREQ-INCOMPLETE: {step_id} declares "{frequency_pattern}" using variable {var} but missing: {missing_conditions}. Frequency control is declared but cannot execute.' All three present → PASS. SKIP for frequency patterns that use inline counters without persistent state (e.g., 'max 3 retries' with retry loop counter). DIMENSION MAPPING AUDIT (C1 v1.18.0 — Doc 32 GEN-3): When evolution introduces custom scoring dimensions (practice scoring, skill assessment, feedback rubrics) alongside existing standard dimensions (growth dimensions, quality dimensions), verify mapping completeness: (1) DETECT DUAL DIMENSION SETS — scan step text for two or more distinct dimension name sets with different naming conventions (e.g., 'logic_coherence' vs 'analysis', 'rebuttal_effectiveness' vs 'communication'). (2) MAPPING CHECK — verify an explicit mapping rule exists converting custom dimensions to standard dimensions (e.g., 'logic_coherence → analysis', 'rebuttal_effectiveness → communication'). (3) SCALE CHECK — verify quantitative scales are compatible or conversion formula exists (e.g., '1-5 score / 5 = 0.0-1.0 strength'). RESULT: dual dimensions detected but no mapping → YELLOW 'GEN3-DIM-UNMAPPED: custom dimension {custom_dim} in {module_a} has no mapping to standard dimension {standard_dim} in {module_b}. Practice/skill scoring cannot integrate into canonical tracking.' SKIP if only one dimension set is used throughout the program.
EvolutionVerify.constraints: Tier-appropriate form. Evolution items must NOT be silently dropped. FIX REGRESSION PROTECTION active. CROSS-MODULE DATA FLOW TRACING (INS-004): After generating cross-module changes, trace each new parameter end-to-end from producer to all consumers. For each new parameter P introduced in module A: (1) verify P appears in A's function output description, (2) for each module B that consumes P, verify P appears in B's parameters or function input description, (3) for multi-hop chains A→B→C, verify intermediary B explicitly documents receiving AND forwarding P. Flag any gap as CRITICAL: 'Data flow gap: parameter {P} not threaded from {source} to {target}'. This check runs BEFORE returning generated files to the pipeline. FLOW ORDERING PRE-CHECK (INS-006): When inserting a new gate node into an existing pipeline Mermaid graph, verify: (1) the gate's FAIL/REJECT path does not route to a node that assumes the gate PASSED, (2) the gate's success path does not skip mandatory upstream validation, (3) if the gate prompts the user, verify no downstream gate can independently deny the same request (avoiding contradictory user experience). Flag ordering conflict as WARNING: 'Flow ordering tension: {new_gate} at position {pos} conflicts with {downstream_gate} assumption'. LOOP SAFETY ANALYSIS (INS-009): For every Mermaid back-edge (a node pointing to an earlier node in the graph, creating a loop), verify: (1) the function body of the loop-entry node contains an explicit termination condition with a finite upper bound (e.g., max_retries, counter, budget), (2) the termination condition is reachable (not guarded by always-true condition), (3) the loop body modifies state that progresses toward termination. Flag missing termination as CRITICAL: 'Unbounded loop: back-edge {from}→{to} has no explicit termination condition with finite bound'.
EvolutionVerify.Error: Verification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: log unverified items, proceed -> inform.

MermaidSync.step1: MERMAID SYNC: Mermaid nodes <-> functions 1:1. All endNode reachable. No orphan edges. MERMAID EDGE COMPLETENESS: for each function, scan steps/constraints for conditional paths (e.g., 'if empty → route to X', 'if NoData → skip to Y'). Each described conditional path MUST have a corresponding edge in the Mermaid graph with a descriptive label. Missing edges → add them. MERMAID EDGE LABEL VALIDATION: for each edge from a routing/NLU/decision node, verify the edge label covers ALL route types described in the corresponding function's step1. SEMANTIC ROUTING CONSISTENCY: for each new path added during evolution, check existing paths with similar operation semantics (create/read/update/delete). If a dedicated node exists for that operation type, new paths MUST route through it (enforces MF8 at generation time).
MermaidSync.step2: EVOLUTION IMPACT SCAN (Evolve only): for each accepted evolution item (LEVEL_A/B/C), trace ALL references across the ENTIRE file: (a) FEATURE COUNT TRACE — scan all functions for hardcoded counts referencing changed lists (Error fields, menus, Explain steps). Update each to match actual count. (b) METADATA TRACE — if version bumped, update name field embedded vX.Y.Z, scan description for stale references. (c) DELEGATION TRACE — if modules added/removed, update NLU routing, delegation functions, Explain lists, menu options. (d) NLU INTENT TRACE (B7 enhanced) — if NLU intents added/removed, execute FULL STANDARD.description_hygiene.nlu_intent_checklist: (d1) confusion pairs — verify each new intent has ≥2 confusion pairs with existing intents, (d2) tiebreaker order — verify new intent is placed in tiebreaker list, (d3) enrichment step — verify NLU enrichment references new intent, (d4) menu/count sync — scan ALL Error fields and description text for hardcoded intent counts (e.g., '11 intents', '12 intent types'), auto-update to match actual count, (d5) Mermaid edges — verify routing node has edge for new intent, (d6) AIAP.md intent_examples — verify at least 1 example per new intent, (d7) node counts in AIAP.md modules — update if nodes added, (d8) Error text counts — scan Error/fallback text for numeric references to changed lists, auto-update. ENFORCEMENT: if any d1-d8 check fails, flag as D4-NLU-STALE with YELLOW severity. B7 makes this checklist MANDATORY (not optional) when NLU changes detected. (e) STANDARD RANGE TRACE: if evolution adds new STANDARD check items (e.g., adding I13 or PL25), scan all AIAP.md body sections and all module description fields for item range references that need updating. For each stale range found, auto-update the upper bound. Example: if I13 was added and description says 'I1-I12', update to 'I1-I13'. Output: impact_trace = { changed_elements[], affected_locations[], all_updated: boolean }. Log in generation_report. COUNT SYNC (all modes): verify all Error/fallback text counts match actual counts. Scan ALL Error fields.
MermaidSync.constraints: All Mermaid nodes must have corresponding functions. All endNode reachable.
MermaidSync.Error: Sync fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: log inconsistencies, proceed to audit -> inform.

MFAudit.step1: MULTI-FILE AUDIT (Pattern B+ only): verify each sub-module's id matches parent's delegation target. Verify shared parameters have consistent types across files. Run full MF1-MF38 checks: MF1 (version consistency), MF2 (delegation target match), MF3 (shared parameter type+default), MF4 (cross-file data contracts — FORMAT/NAME/VALUE/PATH: verify file path templates match across writer/reader modules), MF5 (data_file fallback), MF6 (AIAP.md sync + node count verification + NODE COUNT PRECISION CHECK (CR2 v1.2.0): for each module in AIAP.md modules[], parse the corresponding .aisop.json mermaid field to count actual Mermaid nodes (all '[NodeName]' and '([NodeName])' patterns including Start and endNode). Compare with AIAP.md declared 'nodes: N'. If mismatch: difference ≤ 3 → YELLOW 'MF6-NODE-COUNT: AIAP.md declares {module} nodes={declared} but Mermaid graph has {actual} nodes. Node list: [{node_names}]'; difference > 3 → RED 'MF6-NODE-COUNT-CRITICAL: {module} node count severely mismatched — declared {declared}, actual {actual}. AIAP.md or Mermaid graph may be outdated.' Node counting rules: count all Mermaid node definitions matching regex /\[\w+\]|\(\[\w+\]\)|\(\(\w+\)\)/ — includes decision nodes, process nodes, Start, endNode), MF7 (routing parameter symmetry), MF8 (semantic operation consistency), MF9 (cross-module node invocation — bidirectional: FORWARD check caller→sub-module route exists, REVERSE check sub-module route→has caller), MF10 (system_prompt consistency — all sub-modules include 'Respond in User\'s Language.' and 'Align: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.'), MF11 (Error strategy consistency — all Error fields follow retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform pattern), MF12 (tools union verification — main.tools[] = union of all sub-module tools[]), MF13 (persisted data schema versioning — all file_system write templates include schema_version, all reads handle missing via migration), MF14 (cross-module field consumer validation — trace producer→consumer field references, verify field names/types/semantics match, output dependency graph), MF15 (output contract enforcement — validate module outputs against declared json_schema at pipeline stage boundaries; N/A for contracts without json_schema), MF16 (tool directory consistency — verify tool_dirs references, mcp_server.json exposed_tools match, agent_card hash consistency; Pattern G only, N/A for others), MF17 (mandatory output completeness — verify AIAP.md, quality_baseline.json, agent_card.json exist in target directory; BLOCK if missing per STANDARD). MF24 (protocol document consistency — when LEVEL_A/B changes add new enum values, tools, or behaviors, verify domain protocol document has corresponding coverage; 3+ gaps → RED), MF25 (tool integration completeness — when tools[] modified, verify: (a) at least one module uses added tool, (b) AIAP.md declares it, (c) trust/safety have governance rules if T3+, (d) annotations consistent across modules), MF26 (enum registry consistency — for shared enums across modules, build consolidated registry and verify all consumers cover all authoritative values; gaps/orphans → YELLOW), MF27 (new module dependency symmetry — for LEVEL_A new modules, compare cross-module dependency set against existing modules; missing common dependencies or unique dependencies → YELLOW advisory). MF-INSIGHTS (insights orphan file check — scan target program directory for insights.json and insights.aisop.json via file_system: (a) if insights.json exists but AIAP.md has no 'insights' field → YELLOW 'MF-INSIGHTS-ORPHAN: insights.json found but AIAP.md has no insights field. File may be orphaned from a previous insights removal or manual edit. Recommend: add insights: true to AIAP.md or remove insights.json.' (b) if insights.aisop.json exists but AIAP.md has no 'insights' field → YELLOW 'MF-INSIGHTS-ORPHAN: insights.aisop.json module found but AIAP.md has no insights field. Module will not be activated.' (c) if AIAP.md has insights: true but neither insights.json nor insights.aisop.json exists → PASS (insights field declared but no data yet — normal initial state). (d) if insights.json exists and AIAP.md has insights: true → PASS. (e) if no insights files and no insights field → N/A.). Output: mf_audit = { mf1..mf27: PASS/FAIL, mf_insights: PASS/FAIL/N_A }. Any MF FAIL → log as generation_warning. INCREMENTAL SYNC: if modules were skipped, verify skipped modules' contracts still match regenerated modules' updated contracts. Mismatch → force-regenerate. POST-GENERATE MF PRECHECK (CR8+CR10 v1.2.0, Create mode, Pattern B+): After completing standard MF1-MF38 audit, perform immediate cross-module signal verification: (1) MF20-EARLY: scan all generated module steps for cross-module call patterns ('Call {program}.{module}', 'delegate to {module}', 'pass to {module}'). For each detected call, extract: caller_module, caller_function, caller_step, target_module, target_function, passed_parameters. (2) PARAMETER MATCH: for each cross-module call, load target module's function parameters list. Verify all required parameters of the target function are explicitly passed by the caller. If any required parameter missing: RED 'MF20-EARLY: {caller_module}.{caller_function}.{caller_step} calls {target_module}.{target_function} without required parameter {param}. Fix immediately.' (3) MF1-QUICK: verify all generated .aisop.json files have identical version fields. (4) If any RED found in precheck: apply immediate fixes in Generate1 before passing to Research2. Log fixes as pipeline_metadata.mf_precheck_fixes = [{ issue, file, fix }]. (5) Log: 'MF PRECHECK: {calls_checked} cross-module calls verified, {issues_found} issues ({fixed} auto-fixed).' This catches problems like missing parameters (e.g., RecordInteraction without interaction_result) 3 stages earlier than waiting for ThreeDimTest. (5) PARAMETER NAME ALIGNMENT CHECK (CR1 v1.2.0): for each cross-module call detected in step (1), extract parameter names used by the caller (patterns: '{param_name}=' or comma-separated identifiers in call text). Compare each caller parameter name against the target module's parameters definition keys. MATCHING RULES: (a) exact match → PASS; (b) edit distance ≤ 2 but not exact (e.g., 'message_content' vs 'content_text') → YELLOW 'MF-PRECHECK-NAME-DRIFT: {caller_module}.{caller_step} uses parameter name "{caller_param}" but {target_module} defines "{target_param}" (edit distance {N}). Possible naming convention mismatch.'; (c) no match found in target parameters and no near-match → RED 'MF-PRECHECK-NAME-MISSING: {caller_module}.{caller_step} passes "{caller_param}" to {target_module} but no parameter with that name or similar exists in target definition. Parameter name drift detected.' (d) If RED found: apply immediate auto-fix by mapping to closest target parameter name if edit distance ≤ 4, otherwise block and route to ModifyStep. Log: 'MF PRECHECK NAME ALIGNMENT: {params_checked} parameter names verified, {drift_found} drift issues ({fixed} auto-fixed).' This check applies to BOTH Create and Evolve modes (parameter name drift can occur in either). SKIP: for Pattern A (single module, no cross-module calls).
MFAudit.constraints: Any MF FAIL -> log as generation_warning. Pattern B+ only, skip for Pattern A.
MFAudit.Error: Audit fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip audit with warning -> inform. MF PRECHECK (CR8): cross-module parameter validation failure -> do NOT skip -> RED FAIL with detailed mismatch report: '{caller}.{function}.{step} calls {target}.{function} without required parameter {param}'. Apply immediate auto-fix if parameter source is identifiable, otherwise block Generate1 and route to ModifyStep for manual resolution.

FractalCheck.step1: FRACTAL CHECK per STANDARD.C5 + AIAP_Protocol.md: Count functional nodes per file (total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode). PATTERN CHECK: 2+ independent functions -> suggest Pattern B upgrade. PROGRESSIVE CHECK: 13-15 nodes -> ADVISORY with split suggestions. 16+ -> RECOMMENDED with concrete split plan. Respect fractal_exempt annotation. SHARED CHECK: 2+ modules reuse complex operations -> suggest Pattern C. PATTERN E/F: if plan specifies Pattern E (memory) -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='memory'. If plan specifies Pattern F (ecosystem) -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='orchestrate'. Protocol needs -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='protocol'. SKILL.md export -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='skill_export'. SKILL.md import -> delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='skill_import'. COMPOUND PATTERN COMPOSITION (B+E): when plan specifies both Pattern B (multi-file package) and Pattern E (memory/persistent state), compose as follows: (a) main.aisop.json = NLU router (Pattern B role), (b) functional sub-modules = independent function modules (Pattern B), (c) memory/ directory = persistent state files (Pattern E). DECISION: if 2+ sub-modules need memory access -> create dedicated memory.aisop.json sub-module (Pattern C shared); if only 1 sub-module needs memory -> integrate directly. Delegate to advisor with advisor_type='memory' for memory tier design. Ensure MF4 data contracts cover memory file paths across all memory-accessing modules. Report: 'Pattern {P}, {n} functional nodes, {k} modules.' SUB_MERMAID CHECK per STANDARD.C5.sub_mermaid_decomposition + Protocol §7.1: (1) If file has single main graph with nodes exceeding threshold → check if sub_mermaid decomposition is applicable before recommending file split (P0 priority per split_priority). (2) If file already uses sub_mermaid → verify node counting follows §7.1.4 rules: calculate total_functional, single_path_max, verify fractal_exempt if needed. (3) Verify execution mode is declared in fractal_exempt (mutually_exclusive/sequential/conditional/hybrid). FUNCTION BODY COMPLEXITY CHECK per STANDARD.C5.function_body_complexity: (4) For each function, count steps. 8+ steps → ADVISORY. (5) For each step, count directives (UPPERCASE LABEL: pattern). 15+ → ADVISORY, 20+ → RECOMMENDED. Report: 'sub_mermaid: {applicable/in_use/not_needed}, body_complexity: {max_steps}/{max_directives}.'
FractalCheck.constraints: Fractal check emits progressive warnings, never FAIL on node count alone. Respect fractal_exempt.
FractalCheck.Error: Check fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip check, log advisory -> inform.

ToolingStart.step1: Receive content output (generated .aisop.json files, AIAP.md metadata). Prepare context for tooling generation.
ToolingStart.constraints: If no ui block and no tool_dirs in AIAP.md, both GenerateUI and GenerateToolDirs will skip (conditional execution).
ToolingStart.Error: Invalid input -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip tooling entirely -> inform.

GenerateUI.step1: UI GENERATION (conditional): if target AIAP.md declares a 'ui' block, generate ui_output per STANDARD.data_contracts.ui_output contract. For each component in ui.components: (a) validate data_source references: (i) a known STANDARD data_contract name (e.g., pipeline_metadata, research_context, simulation_report, quality_baseline), OR (ii) a pipeline_metadata sub-field (e.g., pipeline_metadata.token_tracking), OR (iii) a program-internal data source — check target program's module outputs for matching field names or data flow paths (e.g., a module's function output that feeds the ui component via Mermaid data flow). For program-internal sources, verify the producing module exists in AIAP.md modules[] and the referenced data path is traceable through the program's function steps, (b) for type='form', validate fields[] have name+type+default, (b2) for type='form', verify each field name maps to a writable program data path (parameter with file_system persistence or profile field) rather than a runtime-only parameter; flag runtime-only fields as UI_DATA_MISMATCH warning, (c) set rendering_engine from ui.rendering (default 'mcp_apps_v1'), (d) set interactivity=true if any component type='form'. Skip entirely if no ui block (backward compatible). Output ui_output object validated against json_schema.
GenerateUI.constraints: Skip entirely if no ui block (backward compatible). Output ui_output object validated against json_schema.
GenerateUI.Error: UI generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip UI, note in generation_report -> inform.

GenerateToolDirs.step1: TOOL DIRECTORY GENERATION (Pattern G only). If AIAP.md contains tool_dirs field: (a) ANALYZE program tools[] and capabilities to determine required tool implementations, (b) SELECT RUNTIME — recommend language per need: Python (data/AI/ML), TypeScript (Web API/type safety), Go (concurrency/CLI), Rust (performance/WASM), Shell (system scripts/lightweight), (c) GENERATE SKELETON per selected language: README.md (purpose, interface, security constraints), dependency file (requirements.txt / package.json / go.mod / Cargo.toml / none), tool implementation stubs (entry script + function stubs with docstrings), interface definition (CLI args or JSON stdin/stdout), (d) GENERATE MCP ADAPTER — create mcp_tools/mcp_adapter.{ext} (stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 bridge: read stdin line-by-line, parse JSON, route to tool function by params.name, return JSON response, handle errors with code -32601/-32603), (e) GENERATE mcp_server.json — { schema_version: 'mcp-1.0', server_id, transport: 'stdio', runtime, entry_point, exposed_tools: [from tools[]], file_hashes: {SHA-256 per file}, governance: { aiap_protocol, trust_level, governance_hash: '(computed by ReviewStep)' } }, (f) GENERATE agent_card.json (A2A v1.0 optional) — { name, description, capabilities, endpoint }, (g) I13 PRE-CHECK — validate dependency pinning (no ^ or ~ prefixes), network declarations, file scope within tool_dirs/. If non-Pattern-G program: skip with note 'non-Pattern-G, no tool_dirs generation needed.'
GenerateToolDirs.constraints: Pattern G only. If non-Pattern-G program: skip with note. I13 PRE-CHECK required.
GenerateToolDirs.Error: Tool dir generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip tool dirs, note in generation_report -> inform.

ThreeDimTest.step1: TIER DETECTION: Classify target — Simple (Pattern A, no file_system, <=6 functional nodes), Standard (Pattern A with >6 functional nodes OR Pattern B OR uses file_system), Professional (Pattern C/D, multi-file). NOTE: Pattern A with tools=[] and >6 nodes is classified as Standard (complex zero-tool systems have sufficient node count to warrant full testing).
ThreeDimTest.step1_5: FORMAT DETECTION (B1 v1.10.0 — Phase 2 corrected phased evolution plan; JSON Graph Specification jsongraph; JFSM JSON Finite State Machine validation pattern; TypeScript Flow Nodes structural type inference): Detect target program's protocol format for dual-format quality assessment. PURPOSE: enable ThreeDimTest to evaluate AISIP programs using structural inference instead of Mermaid parsing — the 'verify what you generate' principle from Phase 1 type-free generation. RULES: (1) SCAN target directory for file extensions: (a) files matching *.aisop.json AND containing 'graph' or 'flowchart' keyword in aisop object → format includes AISOP, (b) files matching *.aisip.json AND containing 'aisip' top-level key → format includes AISIP. (2) DETERMINE format: (a) AISOP files found AND no AISIP files → format = 'AISOP', (b) AISIP files found AND no AISOP files → format = 'AISIP', (c) both found → format = 'DUAL', (d) neither → ERROR 'No valid AISOP or AISIP files found'. (3) SET pipeline_context.target_format = format. (4) LOG: 'FORMAT DETECT: {format} — {aisop_count} AISOP files, {aisip_count} AISIP files'. (5) DOWNSTREAM ROUTING: format value controls branching in C5 (step2), MF7/MF31 (step6), and AISIP-CHECKS (step6_5). AISOP-only programs: all existing checks run unchanged (backward compatible). AISIP/DUAL programs: additional structural inference checks activated. SKIP: if target_format already set by pipeline context (e.g., from PipelineStart.step_language), use that value instead of re-scanning.
ThreeDimTest.step2: COGNITIVE (C1-C7): C1 archetype fit (STEP REFERENCE DISAMBIGUATION (C1 v1.18.0): in Evolve mode for Pattern B+ programs, collect all step IDs across all modules — if the same step number (e.g., step3_8) appears in 2+ different functions AND both steps are part of the same evolution version's changes, flag advisory YELLOW 'C1-STEP-AMBIGUITY: step {step_id} appears in {function_A} and {function_B} — consider using distinct numbering to reduce cross-reference ambiguity'; non-blocking naming hygiene suggestion only), C2 node load (leaf<=8, convergence<=12), C3 abstraction consistency, C4 information flow, C5 fractal decomposition per AIAP_Protocol.md (functional node count = total - Start - endNode; progressive: 3-12 normal, 13-15 ADVISORY, 16+ MANDATORY SUB_MERMAID FEASIBILITY CHECK (B8); Pattern B+ main = router quality constraint; independence test; split priority: tool boundary > data flow phase > functional independence; C5-SUB_MERMAID-FORCE (B8): when node count >= 16, BEFORE accepting fractal_exempt, perform mandatory sub_mermaid feasibility analysis: (1) SCAN Mermaid graph for independent sub-graphs — identify node clusters with single entry/single exit points, (2) DETECT repeated patterns — 3+ structurally identical branches suggest extractable sub-graph, (3) DETECT functional boundaries — clear phase transitions (e.g., input→process→output) that map to sub_mermaid split points. RESULT: if feasible sub_mermaid decomposition found → RED: 'Module has {N} nodes and {K} decomposable sub-graphs. Must decompose via sub_mermaid before accepting high node count. Suggested splits: {sub_graph_list} with estimated post-split single_path_max={M}.' If NOT feasible (true orchestrator with all mutually exclusive branches, no extractable clusters) → YELLOW accepted with mandatory justification: 'All {N} nodes are mutually exclusive routing branches with no extractable sub-graph.' fractal_exempt is respected ONLY after sub_mermaid feasibility check confirms infeasibility). C5-SUB_MERMAID-DECOMPOSE (PHASE 2 — active decomposition plan generation): When PHASE 1 feasibility analysis finds decomposable sub-graphs (result=RED), MUST generate a concrete sub_mermaid_proposal: (1) PATTERN IDENTIFICATION — classify each decomposable cluster as FAN_OUT (node with 3+ mutually exclusive branches converging to single exit, e.g., intent routing), SEQUENTIAL (linear chain 8+ nodes with clear phase boundaries), or CONDITIONAL (decision node with 2+ substantial branches each containing 3+ nodes). (2) MAIN MERMAID GENERATION — produce new main Mermaid string replacing extracted nodes with SubRef[aisop.<sub_name>] delegation nodes that route to sub-graph and receive result. (3) SUB MERMAID GENERATION — produce new sub_mermaid Mermaid string with Entry routing node dispatching to extracted handler nodes, each handler ending at SubEnd return node. (4) DELEGATE FUNCTION — generate function body for each SubRef node: 'Delegate to aisop.<sub_name> sub-graph. Pass: {input_params}. Receive: {output_params}. Route to next node based on sub-graph result.' (5) ENTRY FUNCTION — generate function body for sub-graph Entry node: routing logic dispatching to correct handler based on delegated parameter. (6) FRACTAL_EXEMPT — generate fractal_exempt text for sub-graph if post-split node count still triggers advisory. (7) ATTACH to test_report as: { check_id: 'C5', severity: 'RED_WITH_FIX', sub_mermaid_proposal: { pattern: FAN_OUT|SEQUENTIAL|CONDITIONAL, main_mermaid: '<new main graph>', sub_mermaids: [{ name: '<sub_name>', mermaid: '<sub graph>', entry_function: {...}, handler_functions: [...] }], delegate_functions: [...], fractal_exempt: '<text or null>', verification: { main_nodes: N, sub_nodes: [M1, M2...], all_under_threshold: boolean } } }. COMPLETENESS RULE: detection without a concrete proposal is INCOMPLETE — B8 check FAILS if it identifies a decomposable graph but does not provide the decomposition. Verification: |V_main| <= 15 AND max(|V_sub_i|) <= 15 for all sub-graphs), C5-AISIP-COUNT (B1 v1.10.0): when target_format == 'AISIP' or 'DUAL', compute AISIP node count using structural inference: node_count = len(aisip.main.keys()), end_nodes = sum(1 for n in aisip.main.values() if n == {}), functional_nodes = node_count - end_nodes. When format == 'DUAL': aisop_count = Mermaid parser count (existing logic), aisip_count = JSON flow key count. If aisop_count != aisip_count → YELLOW 'DUAL node count mismatch: AISOP has {aisop_count} nodes, AISIP has {aisip_count} nodes'. When format == 'AISIP': use structural node_count for all C5 threshold checks (fractal decomposition, sub_mermaid feasibility). C5-FRACTAL-EXEMPT-DRIFT (B3 v1.2.0 — Infrastructure-as-Code drift detection Terraform plan/AWS Config; OpenAPI spec-code sync validation): When a module has fractal_exempt field, verify declared node counts against actual Mermaid graph counts. METHOD: (1) PARSE fractal_exempt string for declared node counts using regex /(\w+)\s+(\d+)/ to extract label-count pairs. (2) COUNT actual nodes in module's Mermaid graphs (main + all sub_mermaid sub-graphs): parse graph string, extract unique node IDs (excluding endNode/End nodes). (3) COMPARE: for each declared label-count pair, match to corresponding sub-graph and compare. If |declared - actual| > 2 → ADVISORY: 'C5-FRACTAL-EXEMPT-DRIFT: {module} fractal_exempt declares {label}={declared}, actual={actual} (delta {delta}). Documentation may be stale.' Score impact: -0.0 (advisory only). If |declared - actual| > 5 → YELLOW: 'C5-FRACTAL-EXEMPT-DRIFT: {module} significant node count drift — declared {declared}, actual {actual}. fractal_exempt text needs update.' Score impact: -0.2 to C5. (4) TOTAL VERIFICATION: sum all declared counts, compare with actual total. Report drift percentage. (5) Record: fractal_exempt_drift = { modules_checked: int, drifts_found: int, max_delta: int, details: [{ module, label, declared, actual, delta, severity }] }. SKIP if module has no fractal_exempt field (standard C5 scoring applies). C6 brain principle (LLM-native vs tool-required), C7 token budget compliance (per STANDARD C7 — verify AIAP.md runtime.token_budget is set and modules declare expected complexity). sub_mermaid decomposition check per Protocol §7.1 (verify node counting uses single_path_max, execution mode declared, fractal_exempt format correct); function body complexity check per STANDARD.C5.function_body_complexity (steps_per_function and directives_per_step thresholds)
ThreeDimTest.step2_5: TOOL-COMPUTED CHECKS (B3 v1.15.0): When file_system tool is available, the following scoring items MUST be computed via tool rather than LLM estimation. auto_compute=true items produce deterministic, reproducible scores. (1) C7-TOKEN-COUNT: read each .aisop.json file via file_system, compute file_size_bytes, estimate token_count = file_size_bytes / 4 (chars/4 approximation). Compare with AIAP.md runtime.token_budget if declared. Report: { file, chars, estimated_tokens, budget, within_budget: bool }. Score: within budget → 5, within 1.5x → 4, exceeds 1.5x → 3. (2) D6-GOVERNANCE-HASH: read all .aisop.json files (sorted alphabetically), CRLF→LF normalize, compute SHA-256. Compare with AIAP.md governance_hash field. PASS (hash matches) → 5, FAIL (mismatch or missing) → 2. Report: { computed_hash, declared_hash, match: bool }. (3) C5-ERROR-COVERAGE: scan each function node in all .aisop.json files, count nodes_with_error (has 'Error' key) and total_function_nodes. coverage_ratio = nodes_with_error / total_function_nodes. Score: >=0.8 → 5, >=0.6 → 4, >=0.4 → 3, <0.4 → 2. Report: { total_nodes, nodes_with_error, coverage_ratio }. (4) C6-CONSTRAINT-COVERAGE: scan each function node, count nodes_with_constraints (has 'constraints' key) and total_function_nodes. coverage_ratio = nodes_with_constraints / total_function_nodes. Score: >=0.7 → 5, >=0.5 → 4, >=0.3 → 3, <0.3 → 2. Report: { total_nodes, nodes_with_constraints, coverage_ratio }. MERGE: tool-computed scores override LLM-estimated scores for the same check items. Include auto_compute_report in test_report for transparency. FALLBACK: if file_system unavailable (e.g., standalone validation without tools), fall back to LLM estimation with note: 'auto_compute=false (file_system unavailable)'.
ThreeDimTest.step3: INTRINSIC (I1-I13): I1 tool honesty (no phantom/undeclared), I2 security (5 guards; 4 guards if no file_system; prompt-injection focus if tools=[] per STANDARD I2 ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION; per STANDARD tier_rules), I3 data integrity (atomic writes; N/A if tools=[] per STANDARD I3 EXEMPTION), I4 circuit-breaker (named fallbacks; NEW STEP ERROR COVERAGE (B4 v1.18.0): in Evolve mode, when a new step is added to an existing function, verify the function's Error field mentions the new step's failure mode — if Error field was NOT updated to cover the new step, flag YELLOW 'I4-STEP-UNCOVERED: new step {step_id} in {function} not covered by Error field — suggest adding step-specific fallback or step-level Error annotation'; for steps introducing new data outputs, verify Error field specifies failure handling for that output — null, empty array, or default value; SKIP for Create mode or when no new steps added to existing functions), I5 tool suitability (check Symbolic/Delegation/Zero-tool/Auto-Recognition exemptions per STANDARD I5), I6 resilience + semantic accuracy (structural: every function has Error field with retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform pattern; semantic: extract numbers from each Error/fallback text, cross-reference against actual counts in same function's steps — e.g., 'list all N features' where N must equal actual feature list length; flag mismatches as I6 FAIL), I7 parameter completeness + metadata consistency (parameters: each has type + agentic_prompt and is referenced in functions; metadata: if name contains vX.Y.Z must match version field; id module-name must match filename stem), I8 threat model awareness (for T3/T4 trust_level: verify Start.constraints address applicable threats from AT1-AT6 per STANDARD.security threat_taxonomy based on tools[] and trust_level; minimum: all programs address AT6, T3+ address AT4, network-tool programs address AT3; cross-reference per STANDARD.security I8), I9 supply chain integrity (for published/external programs: verify governance_hash; for trust_level >= T3: governance_hash REQUIRED per STANDARD I9), I10 trust level compliance (per STANDARD I10 — verify declared trust_level matches actual permissions and side_effects; flag over-privileged or under-declared), I11 program identity verification (per STANDARD.security I11 — for published programs with status='active' or trust_level >= T3: verify AIAP.md identity block contains valid program_id format and non-empty publisher; if signature present, verify against governance_hash), I12 tool annotation consistency (per STANDARD I12 — I12 TOOL ANNOTATION CONSISTENCY — PER-MODULE GRANULARITY: for Pattern B+ programs, I12 check operates at module level, not program level. For each module: (a) identify tools actually listed in that module's tools[] array, (b) verify those tools' annotations (read_only, destructive, idempotent) are consistent with that module's declared side_effects, (c) zero-tool modules (tools[] empty or absent) are automatically EXEMPT from I12 — score as N/A and exclude from denominator. This prevents false positives where a program-level tool declaration mismatches a module that doesn't use that tool. For non-Pattern-B+ programs: verify read_only matches module side_effects, destructive requires T3+, open_world requires AT3 mitigation, idempotent matches module declaration; N/A for programs without tool annotations), I13 embedded code safety (per STANDARD I13 — verify tool_dirs/ code passes integrity, network, file scope, dependency, MCP proxy, and shell audit checks (10 sub-items per STANDARD I13); when aiap_tools/ present, also verify (g) AIAP trust ceiling — sub trust_level <= parent, (h) AIAP permission subset — sub permissions within parent scope, (i) AIAP recursion depth — nesting <= 3, (j) AIAP governance hash — each sub-program hash verified; N/A for Pattern A-F). I-SELF-REFERENTIAL SCORING (B1 v1.8.0 — Stanford 'Law of Evaluation' 2025 self-evaluating system bias; Partnership on AI 2026 agent evaluation sequences; Frontiers 2025 self-governing systems circular evaluation): When the target program being evaluated IS the Creator itself (detected by: AIAP.md name == 'aiap_creator' OR AIAP.md id contains 'aiap_creator'), I8-I13 MUST NOT be marked N/A if the program actually possesses the features being tested. SELF-REFERENTIAL CHECK: (a) I8 — Creator has AT1-AT6 threat taxonomy in simulate.aisop.json and AIAP_Standard.security → APPLICABLE (not N/A).
