(b) I9 — Creator has governance_hash in AIAP.md and trust_level=T4 → APPLICABLE. (c) I10 — Creator has trust_level declaration → APPLICABLE. (d) I11 — Creator has identity block with program_id → APPLICABLE. (e) I12 — Creator has 3 tools with annotations → APPLICABLE. (f) I13 — Creator is Pattern D+G with AIAP_Tools (when present) → APPLICABLE. RULE: for self-referential programs, each I-item's N/A determination must be based SOLELY on whether the feature exists in the target, NOT on self-referential status. If Creator has governance_hash → I9 is scored, not skipped. If Creator has AT1-AT6 coverage → I8 is scored, not skipped. This eliminates the bootstrapping bias where a self-evaluating system inflates its own score by reducing the denominator. OUTPUT: applicable_count must reflect actual features present. For Creator with all features: applicable_count = 13 (not 7). SKIP: non-self-referential programs (standard N/A logic applies unchanged). PROVISIONAL STATE: if pipeline_active=true (mid-pipeline execution where AIAP.md identity fields may not yet be finalized — e.g., verified_on not updated until ReviewStep), downgrade I11 missing-identity findings from WARNING to INFO with note 'identity fields provisional during pipeline execution — will be finalized in ReviewStep'. I2 INHERITANCE: for Pattern B+ sub-modules, if a guard is annotated as '(inherited from main router)' or '(inherited from main)', verify the main router file's Start.constraints contains the corresponding full guard implementation. If main has it → count as PASS for the sub-module. If main lacks it → count as FAIL (broken inheritance chain). Non-inheritable guards (A: Type guard, D: Path guard) must always be present locally in each sub-module — these require module-specific knowledge and cannot be inherited.
ThreeDimTest.step4: DETAIL (D1-D10): D1 step clarity (concrete verbs), D2 format consistency (step keys must follow sequential integer naming step1/step2/.../stepN; non-standard suffixes like step1_5/step2b/step4c are permitted for insertion ordering but count as minor format inconsistency scoring 4 not 5), D3 null propagation, D4 consistency + internal coherence (FORMAT: CamelCase Mermaid nodes, snake_case params; SEMANTIC: steps must not contradict own constraints; REFERENCE: any number mentioned in text must match actual count — menu items, feature lists, module counts; CROSS-FUNCTION: if function A names function B in text, B must exist in same file; CONSTRAINT CROSS-VALIDATION: for each module, compare constraints across ALL functions for logical consistency — (a) if any function declares 'Read-only' in constraints, no other function in same module may perform write operations; (b) if routing function declares 'Exactly one route', same function must not describe multiple simultaneous routes; (c) Mermaid edge count from decision node must match route count in function logic). D4-STANDARD-REF INTEGRITY CHECK (B4, automated): scan all constraints and description fields across loaded AISOP files for 'per STANDARD.{section_name}' or 'per STANDARD {section_name}' references. For each reference, extract section_name and verify it exists as a key in the loaded STANDARD files (core/security/ecosystem/performance). If referenced section does not exist → YELLOW: 'Constraint references STANDARD.{section_name} which does not exist in loaded STANDARD files. Possible stale reference from prior STANDARD version.' D4-RANGE CROSS-REFERENCE CHECK (automated): scan all description fields across loaded AISOP files AND AIAP.md body text for item range patterns matching regex /[A-Z]+\d+-[A-Z]*\d+/ (e.g., 'C1-C7', 'I1-I13', 'PL1-PL27', 'MF1-MF38', 'QRG1-QRG5', 'AT1-AT6'). For each detected range, extract the prefix and upper bound. Cross-reference against loaded STANDARD quality_checklist item counts: C items (C1-C7), I items (I1-I13), D items (D1-D10), MF items (MF1-MF38), PL items (PL1-PL27), QRG items (QRG1-QRG5). If detected upper bound differs from STANDARD actual count (e.g., 'I1-I12' when STANDARD has I1-I13), flag as D4-RANGE-STALE with YELLOW severity: 'Range reference [found_range] is stale — STANDARD has [correct_range]'. D4-GOVERNANCE-FIELD VALIDATION (B1 v1.7.0 — Singapore IMDA Agentic AI Framework 2026 boundary condition enforcement; NIST AI RMF + ISO 42001 integrated governance validation; AIGOV@AAAI 2026 runtime invariant verification): scan AIAP.md frontmatter for governance-critical fields and verify values against AIAP Protocol V1.0.0 standard definitions. PURPOSE: catch governance field errors (typos, wrong values, semantic confusion) that undermine protocol compliance — discovered empirically when aisop_auto_evolution had axiom_0='Human_Sovereignty_and_Benefit' instead of 'Human_Sovereignty_and_Wellbeing'. CHECKS: (a) AXIOM_0: value MUST be exactly 'Human_Sovereignty_and_Wellbeing'. If different → YELLOW D4-GOVERNANCE-AXIOM: 'AIAP.md axiom_0 is "{actual}", expected "Human_Sovereignty_and_Wellbeing". This is a protocol-defined immutable value.' Common errors: 'Benefit' instead of 'Wellbeing', 'human_sovereignty' lowercase, missing underscores. (b) GOVERNANCE_MODE: value MUST be one of ['NORMAL', 'STRICT']. Unknown value → YELLOW D4-GOVERNANCE-MODE: 'governance_mode "{value}" not recognized. Expected: NORMAL or STRICT.' (c) PROTOCOL FIELD FORMAT: value MUST match pattern 'AIAP V{digits}.{digits}.{digits}'. Non-matching → YELLOW D4-GOVERNANCE-PROTOCOL: 'protocol field "{value}" does not match expected format "AIAP V{major}.{minor}.{patch}".' (d) AUTHORITY/SEED/EXECUTOR DOMAIN: each MUST be a valid domain format (contains at least one dot, no spaces). Invalid → YELLOW D4-GOVERNANCE-DOMAIN: '{field} value "{value}" is not a valid domain.' Report: governance_field_validation = { fields_checked: 6, fields_valid: int, issues: [{ field, expected, actual, severity }] }. SCORING: each YELLOW issue contributes -0.2 to D4 item score (minimum D4 score = 3). D5 boundary rules, D6 multilingual, D7 metabolism (no dead code). D8 CHECK: verify AIAP.md exists in target directory. Check 14 required frontmatter fields. Check 3 required body sections. Check closing seal. For Pattern A: modules list must contain exactly one entry per STANDARD D8 pattern_a_note. Score: PASS if all present, FAIL if missing. D8-LICENSE SEMANTIC CHECK: if AIAP.md contains a license field, validate: (a) value is a recognized SPDX identifier (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0-only, GPL-2.0-only, BSD-2-Clause, BSD-3-Clause, ISC, MPL-2.0, LGPL-3.0-only, AGPL-3.0-only, Unlicense, CC0-1.0, CC-BY-4.0, CC-BY-SA-4.0) or 'proprietary'. (b) value does NOT match protocol version pattern (reject 'AIAP V*' as license value — this is a common semantic error of confusing protocol version with license). (c) if license='proprietary', verify terms_url or contact field exists in AIAP.md. Score: valid SPDX → pass, invalid/missing for Store programs → YELLOW, protocol version in license field → YELLOW with specific message 'license field contains protocol version, not SPDX identifier'. D8-SKILLS: verify skills[] in AIAP.md matches main.aisop.json NLU intent count — YELLOW if mismatch. D8-AGENT-CARD: verify agent_card.json exists and fields match AIAP.md frontmatter — YELLOW if stale or fields mismatch. D8-AGENT-CARD-MISSING: Applies to ALL programs. Check: (1) agent_card.json exists, (2) agent_card.json contains safety_card object with required fields (risk_level, data_handling, limitations). Missing agent_card → -1.0 (RED). Missing safety_card → -0.5 (YELLOW). D8-AGENT-CARD-ENFORCE (B5): For Pattern F/G programs, agent_card.json is MANDATORY — missing agent_card is RED (not recoverable by auto-generation during ThreeDimTest; must be generated during GenerateToolDirs or PrepareAIAPMd). D8-SUMMARY (B1): Check AIAP.md summary field character count. If summary > 500 characters → YELLOW with message 'summary field exceeds 500-character guideline ({actual_length} chars). Consider condensing.' This promotes the ≤500 char advisory from quality_baseline.json Y3 to an automated check. D9 lifecycle status validity: verify AIAP.md status is one of draft/active/deprecated/archived. If deprecated: verify deprecated_date and successor fields exist. If archived: verify no Evolve/Modify operations attempted. D6/D9 CREATE MODE FAIR SCORING (CR11 v1.2.0): In Create mode (pipeline_context indicates old_version_context is null): (1) D6 governance_hash check: if AIAP.md governance_hash field contains 'pending' placeholder (per CR9), score D6 as 5 (full compliance) with note 'D6 governance_hash deferred to ReviewStep — standard for CREATE mode'. Do NOT deduct points for placeholder value. (2) D9 governance_hash storage: in Create mode, governance_hash has not yet been computed, so D9 check for 'hash stored in individual files' is N/A. Score D9 as 5 with note 'D9 deferred to ReviewStep — standard for CREATE mode'. (3) Report: 'CREATE MODE SCORING: D6, D9 deferred to ReviewStep (governance_hash not yet computed). Scores reflect deferred status, not missing data.' This ensures CREATE mode programs are not penalized for the natural timing gap between AIAP.md generation (Generate1) and governance_hash computation (ReviewStep). EVOLVE MODE: D6/D9 scoring unchanged — governance_hash must be present and valid. D10 applicability condition completeness (per STANDARD D10 — verify AIAP.md applicability_condition exists with non-empty triggers; pipeline_exemption: deferred during Create pipeline).
ThreeDimTest.step4_5: PRE-EXISTING VS NEW ISSUE SEPARATION (C1 v1.14.0): For Evolve mode (when old_version_context is non-null and .evolution_snapshot exists), after completing all dimension checks, classify each found issue as PRE-EXISTING or NEW. METHOD: (1) Load .evolution_snapshot/ snapshot files (the pre-evolution baseline). (2) For each issue found in the current version, check if the same issue category + location existed in the snapshot version. MATCHING RULES: same check ID (e.g., 'C2', 'I7', 'D2') + same file + same function → PRE-EXISTING. Issue in a NEW step/function (added by this evolution) → NEW. Issue in an existing function but different check ID → NEW. (3) Report separately: 'PRE-EXISTING: {N} issues (carried from v{old_version}) | NEW: {M} issues (introduced in this evolution)'. (4) SCORING IMPACT: both pre-existing and new issues affect the dimension score equally (no discount for pre-existing). However, the separation enables: (a) regression detection — if new issues > 0, flag 'Evolution introduced {M} new quality issues', (b) improvement tracking — if pre-existing issues decreased, note 'Evolution resolved {K} pre-existing issues', (c) review prioritization — reviewers can focus on NEW issues first. (5) Include in test_report: issue_classification: { pre_existing: [{ check_id, file, description }], new: [{ check_id, file, description }], resolved: [{ check_id, file, was_in_snapshot: true }] }. SKIP: for Create mode (no snapshot), all issues are classified as NEW.
ThreeDimTest.step4_7: DIMENSION REGRESSION ALERT (B3 v1.3.0 — quality trend hardening): DEPENDENCY: requires step4_5 score_trajectory output. If step4_5 was skipped (Create mode), this step is also skipped. For Evolve mode, after computing score_trajectory in step4_5, perform CONSECUTIVE REGRESSION DETECTION: (1) Load .version_history/ for the 3 most recent quality_baseline.json snapshots (current + 2 prior). (2) For each dimension (C, I, D), extract scores across the 3 versions and compute deltas. (3) If any single dimension shows CONSECUTIVE DECLINE across 3+ versions with cumulative drop > 0.10: emit DIMENSION_REGRESSION_ALERT: 'ALERT: {dimension} has declined for 3 consecutive evolutions ({v1}: {s1} → {v2}: {s2} → {v3}: {s3}, cumulative Δ={delta}). Root causes: [{check_ids}]. Recommend targeted hardening before further evolution.' (4) If cumulative drop > 0.30 for any dimension: escalate to DIMENSION_REGRESSION_CRITICAL and include in test_report as RED item — sustained quality erosion indicates systemic issue. (5) Include in test_report: consecutive_regression: { checked: boolean, dimensions_declining: [{ dimension, versions: [{version, score}], cumulative_delta, alert_level: 'none'|'alert'|'critical' }] }. SKIP: for Create mode (no history), or when < 3 version snapshots available.
ThreeDimTest.step4_8: SEMANTIC REGRESSION DETECTION (B1 v1.4.0 — Meta AI NAACL 2024 Semantic Drift Score; Arize embedding drift detection; Confident AI regression testing framework): For Evolve mode only (old_version_context is non-null and .evolution_snapshot/ exists). After pre-existing/new issue separation (step4_5) and dimension regression alert (step4_7), perform SEMANTIC DIFF on all functions that were NOT declared as modified in evolution_plan. PURPOSE: detect unintentional semantic changes in steps that should have remained unchanged — threshold drift, logic changes, output field modifications that slip through without being declared as part of the evolution. METHOD: (1) SNAPSHOT LOAD: read each .aisop.json from .evolution_snapshot/ directory. Parse all function steps into semantic_elements map: { module_id: { function_name: { step_id: { thresholds: [float values extracted via regex /\d+\.\d+|\b\d{2,}\b/], conditions: [keywords: 'if', 'when', 'AND', 'OR', 'unless', 'except', 'only when', 'must', 'MUST NOT', 'SKIP'], output_fields: [identifiers after 'output', 'emit', 'pass', 'return', 'set', 'store', 'record', 'log'], tool_calls: [identifiers after 'file_system', 'google_search', 'web_browser', 'delegate to'], enum_values: [quoted strings in constraint lists] } } } }. (2) CURRENT LOAD: extract same semantic_elements from current (post-evolution) files. (3) DIFF: for each step NOT listed in evolution_plan.changes (steps that should be unchanged): compare snapshot vs current semantic_elements. (4) CLASSIFICATION: (a) THRESHOLD DRIFT — any float threshold value changed (e.g., 0.6→0.3): RED 'SEMANTIC-THRESHOLD-DRIFT: {module}.{function}.{step} threshold changed from {old} to {new} without evolution_plan declaration.' (b) CONDITION CHANGE — condition keywords added/removed: YELLOW 'SEMANTIC-CONDITION-DRIFT: {module}.{function}.{step} conditional logic modified without declaration.' (c) OUTPUT FIELD CHANGE — output field names added/removed: YELLOW 'SEMANTIC-OUTPUT-DRIFT: {module}.{function}.{step} output fields changed. Downstream consumers may be affected.' (d) TOOL CALL CHANGE — tool references changed: RED 'SEMANTIC-TOOL-DRIFT: {module}.{function}.{step} tool usage changed. Security and trust implications.' (e) ENUM VALUE CHANGE — enum values modified: YELLOW 'SEMANTIC-ENUM-DRIFT: {module}.{function}.{step} enum values changed.' (5) REPORT: semantic_regression: { steps_scanned, steps_unchanged, steps_drifted, drifts: [{ module, function, step, drift_type, old_value, new_value, severity }] }. If any RED drift → traffic_report.red[]. (6) FALSE POSITIVE GUARD: whitespace-only, comment, formatting changes are NOT drift — ignore. Version string updates ('v1.3.0'→'v1.4.0' in descriptions) are expected and exempt. SKIP: Create mode (no snapshot), or evolution_plan contains '*' wildcard (full rewrite).
ThreeDimTest.step4_9: CROSS-VERSION SEMANTIC TREND ANALYSIS (B2 v1.5.0 — AtScale 2025 'AI amplified semantic drift'; Mike Mason 2026 'architectural drift from locally sensible but globally inconsistent decisions'; emergentmind.com semantic drift measurement via cosine distance and KL divergence): DEPENDENCY: requires step4_8 semantic_regression output. If step4_8 was skipped (Create mode), this step is also skipped. For Evolve mode, after single-version semantic diff (step4_8), perform MULTI-VERSION SEMANTIC TREND ANALYSIS across the 3 most recent evolution snapshots. PURPOSE: detect slow, cumulative semantic drift that individual single-version diffs miss — the 'boiling frog' problem where each evolution makes small acceptable changes that compound into significant divergence. METHOD: (1) HISTORY LOAD: load semantic_elements maps from .version_history/ for the 3 most recent versions (or .evolution_snapshot/ + .evolution_snapshot_v{prev}/ if .version_history/ unavailable). If < 3 versions available, skip trend analysis (insufficient history). (2) THRESHOLD TREND DETECTION: for each step that exists across all 3 versions, extract threshold values. If a threshold shows MONOTONIC CHANGE across 3 versions (e.g., 0.8 → 0.7 → 0.6, consistently decreasing): emit THRESHOLD_EROSION_ALERT: 'TREND: {module}.{function}.{step} threshold {name} has monotonically {decreased|increased} across 3 versions: {v1}={val1} → {v2}={val2} → {v3}={val3}. Cumulative drift: {total_delta}. Risk: gradual quality/safety erosion.' Severity: cumulative_delta > 0.3 → YELLOW, cumulative_delta > 0.5 → RED. (3) CONDITION COMPLEXITY GROWTH: for each function across 3 versions, count condition keywords (if/when/AND/OR/unless/except/must/SKIP). If count shows CONSISTENT GROWTH across 3 versions (e.g., 5 → 8 → 12): emit COMPLEXITY_CREEP_ALERT: 'TREND: {function} condition complexity growing: {v1}={c1} → {v2}={c2} → {v3}={c3} condition keywords. Risk: increasing cognitive load, potential for contradictory conditions.' Severity: growth_rate > 50% cumulative → YELLOW, growth_rate > 100% → RED. (4) OUTPUT FIELD BLOAT: for each function across 3 versions, count unique output_fields. If count consistently grows: emit OUTPUT_BLOAT_ALERT: 'TREND: {function} output fields growing: {v1}={n1} → {v2}={n2} → {v3}={n3}. Risk: expanding API surface without deprecation, downstream consumer confusion.' Severity: always YELLOW (advisory). (5) AGGREGATE: semantic_trend = { versions_analyzed: int, threshold_erosions: [ErosionEntry], complexity_creeps: [CreepEntry], output_bloats: [BloatEntry], trend_health: 'healthy' (0 alerts) | 'drifting' (1-3 alerts) | 'critical' (4+ alerts or any RED) }. Include in test_report. (6) TREND HEALTH GATE: if trend_health = 'critical', include in traffic_report as YELLOW mandatory-review item (not blocking, but flagged for ReviewStep attention). SKIP: Create mode, or < 3 version snapshots available.
ThreeDimTest.step5_5: FRAMEWORK COMPLETENESS AUDIT (B5 v1.4.0 — Anthropic evals methodology structured evaluation; Confident AI continuous validation; IBM Agentic Drift observability framework): After per-item scoring and before multi-file aggregate, perform three structural completeness checks that verify the target program's internal consistency beyond what individual C/I/D checks catch. PURPOSE: detect gaps where the program DECLARES capabilities but doesn't IMPLEMENT them, or IMPLEMENTS error-prone operations but doesn't PROTECT them. (1) ERROR COVERAGE COMPLETENESS: for each function in each module, scan all steps for operations that can fail: (a) file_system operations (read/write/delete), (b) delegation calls ('delegate to {module}'), (c) external tool calls (google_search, web_browser, etc.), (d) JSON parsing operations ('parse', 'JSON.parse', 'load'), (e) conditional checks that branch on external data. For each failible operation found, verify the function's Error field mentions a failure handling path for that operation type. SCORING: count failible_operations and covered_operations. Coverage ratio = covered / failible. If ratio < 0.7 → YELLOW: 'ERROR-COVERAGE-GAP: {function} has {failible} failible operations but Error field covers only {covered}. Uncovered: [{uncovered_ops}].' If ratio < 0.4 → RED: 'ERROR-COVERAGE-CRITICAL: {function} has major gaps in error handling.' If ratio >= 0.7 → PASS. (2) CONSTRAINT-ENFORCEMENT BIDIRECTIONAL ALIGNMENT (B3 v1.5.0 enhanced — Design Healing framework automated compliance; PHPArkitect architectural constraint verification; PADL 2026 declarative constraint programming): FORWARD CHECK (constraint→implementation): for each function, extract all constraint declarations from the constraints field (e.g., 'Max 3 retries', 'Read-only access', 'Must validate input', 'Never auto-approve'). For each constraint, scan function steps for enforcement evidence — steps that implement the constraint. MATCHING: constraint 'Max N retries' → step mentioning 'retry_count' or 'circuit-breaker' or 'max retries'. Constraint 'Read-only' → no steps performing write operations. Constraint 'Must validate' → step with 'guard', 'check', 'verify', 'validate'. Constraint 'Never auto-X' → no step performing X without human gate. If constraint has no enforcement evidence → YELLOW: 'CONSTRAINT-UNENFORCED: {function}.constraints declares "{constraint}" but no step implements enforcement.' REVERSE CHECK (implementation→constraint): scan all function steps for enforcement patterns that are NOT declared in constraints. Detect: (a) retry/circuit-breaker patterns in steps without 'Max N retries' in constraints → ADVISORY 'CONSTRAINT-UNDECLARED: {function}.{step} implements retry logic but constraints field does not declare retry limit.', (b) validation guards in steps without corresponding constraint declaration → ADVISORY 'CONSTRAINT-UNDECLARED: {function}.{step} performs {guard_type} validation but constraints field does not declare this requirement.', (c) human gates/approvals in steps without 'Never auto-X' constraint → ADVISORY 'CONSTRAINT-UNDECLARED: {function}.{step} requires human approval but constraints field does not declare manual gate requirement.' QUANTITATIVE MATCH: extract numbers from constraint text (regex /\b(\d+)\b/ in context of 'Max', 'minimum', 'at least', 'up to', 'limit'). For each extracted number, scan steps for the same quantitative value in matching context. If constraint says 'Max 3 retries' but step says 'retry_count < 5' → YELLOW 'CONSTRAINT-VALUE-MISMATCH: {function} constraints declare Max 3 retries but {step} enforces limit of 5.' CROSS-FUNCTION CONSTRAINT PROPAGATION: if function A's constraints declare requirements about delegation results (e.g., 'delegate result must be validated', 'callee must return within timeout'), check that functions receiving delegation from A have corresponding validation/timeout steps. Missing → YELLOW 'CONSTRAINT-PROPAGATION-GAP: {function_A} constraints require "{requirement}" from callee {function_B} but {function_B} has no corresponding enforcement.' CROSS-MODULE CONSTRAINT PROPAGATION (B3 v1.6.0 — EuroSys'25 Multi-Grained Specifications distributed invariant verification; Basilisk provenance-based invariant derivation via Atomic Sharding; Scalable Formal Verification of Modular Concurrent Systems): For Pattern B+ programs with 2+ modules, extend constraint propagation from same-file to cross-module scope. METHOD: (a) CROSS-MODULE CONSTRAINT EXTRACTION: for each module file (.aisop.json), extract all constraints fields from all functions. Build module_constraints_map: { module_id: [{ function, constraint_text, constraint_type: 'safety'|'data'|'flow'|'limit' }] }. Classify constraints by type: safety = contains 'never'/'must not'/'forbidden'/'block'/'reject'; data = contains 'validate'/'format'/'schema'/'type'; flow = contains 'delegate'/'call'/'pass to'/'route'; limit = contains 'max'/'min'/'limit'/'timeout'/'quota'. (b) PROPAGATION CHAIN BUILDING: parse Mermaid graphs across all modules to identify cross-module call relationships (patterns: 'delegate to {module}', 'Call {program}.{module}', 'pass to {module}'). Build directed propagation_graph: { caller_module → callee_module → [delegation_steps] }. For multi-hop chains (A→B→C), verify intermediary module B explicitly receives AND forwards constraint-relevant parameters. (c) PROVENANCE INVARIANT VERIFICATION (inspired by Basilisk Atomic Sharding): for each constraint that involves cross-module data flow, verify the INVARIANT CHAIN — the constraint must be enforced at EVERY module in the propagation path, not just origin and destination. Example: if module A declares 'input must be sanitized' and delegates to B which delegates to C, verify B does not strip sanitization metadata before forwarding to C. Detection: extract constraint-relevant fields at each hop; if field present at hop N but absent at hop N+1 → RED 'CONSTRAINT-PROVENANCE-BREAK: invariant "{constraint}" from {module_A} lost at {module_B} — field {field} not forwarded to {module_C}.' (d) SAFETY CONSTRAINT ESCALATION: constraints classified as 'safety' type receive special handling. If a safety constraint in module A (e.g., 'Must reject harmful content') has no corresponding enforcement in ANY downstream module that processes the same data → RED (not YELLOW): 'SAFETY-CONSTRAINT-GAP: safety constraint "{constraint}" in {module_A} has no enforcement in downstream {module_B}. Safety constraints require full propagation chain coverage.' Safety gaps are non-waivable and must be resolved before evolution acceptance. (e) REPORT: cross_module_propagation = { modules_scanned: int, constraints_extracted: int, propagation_chains: [{ from_module, to_module, via_steps, constraints_checked }], chain_gaps: [{ chain_path, missing_module, constraint_text, severity }], safety_gaps: [{ constraint_text, origin_module, uncovered_modules, severity: 'RED' }], provenance_breaks: [{ invariant, break_point, field_lost }], cross_module_coverage: float }. If safety_gaps.length > 0 → include in traffic_report.red[]. SKIP: for Pattern A (single module), or programs with only 1 .aisop.json file. Report: { constraints_declared: int, constraints_enforced: int, unenforced: [{ function, constraint_text, suggested_enforcement }], undeclared_enforcements: [{ function, step, enforcement_pattern }], value_mismatches: [{ function, constraint_value, step_value, context }], propagation_gaps: [{ caller, callee, requirement }], cross_module_propagation: { modules_scanned, constraints_extracted, propagation_chains, chain_gaps, safety_gaps, provenance_breaks, cross_module_coverage }, forward_coverage: float, reverse_coverage: float }. (3) DESCRIPTION-IMPLEMENTATION ALIGNMENT: read module description and summary fields. Extract capability claims (noun phrases describing features). For each claimed capability, search function steps for implementation evidence. If a capability is claimed in description but has zero implementation steps → YELLOW: 'DESCRIPTION-PHANTOM: module {module} description claims "{capability}" but no function step implements it.' If implementation exists but capability is NOT mentioned in description → ADVISORY (not scored): 'DESCRIPTION-UNDOCUMENTED: {function}.{step} implements "{capability}" not mentioned in description.' Report: { capabilities_claimed: int, capabilities_implemented: int, phantom_capabilities: [{ module, capability_text }], undocumented_implementations: [{ module, function, step, capability_text }] }. AGGREGATE: framework_completeness = { error_coverage: { ratio, issues }, constraint_enforcement: { ratio, issues }, description_alignment: { ratio, issues } }. Include in test_report. Overall framework_completeness_score = average of three ratios (0.0-1.0). If < 0.6 → include as Intrinsic dimension modifier: I_score -= 0.3. If < 0.4 → I_score -= 0.6. SKIP: for Simple tier programs (Pattern A, <=6 nodes) — insufficient complexity to warrant framework audit.
ThreeDimTest.step5: PER-ITEM SCORING (1-5): Score each check item individually — 5=Full compliance (exemplary), 4=Minor issue (cosmetic/documentation), 3=Moderate issue (functional but improvable), 2=Significant issue (partially non-compliant), 1=Critical issue (missing/fundamentally broken). Dimension score = average of applicable item scores. GRADE THRESHOLDS: S(weighted>=4.50), A(>=3.50), B(>=2.50), C(>=1.50), D(<1.50). Per-dimension grades: A(avg>=4.50), B(>=3.50), C(>=2.50), D(<2.50). DYNAMIC WEIGHTING per STANDARD.dynamic_weights: if tier=='Simple', use C:0.35 I:0.35 D:0.30; if tier=='Standard' or 'Professional', use C:0.25 I:0.45 D:0.30. N/A HANDLING: when I8, I9, I10, I11, I12, and/or I13 are N/A (non-published programs, no external dependencies, no trust_level declared, no tool_dirs), exclude from Intrinsic dimension denominator — calculate I score as sum(applicable_scores) / count(applicable_items). Weighted score = C*Cw + I*Iw + D*Dw where Cw/Iw/Dw are tier-specific weights. AUTOMATED/SUBJECTIVE SPLIT: classify each check item per STANDARD automated check classification. Run automated checks first (D2, D4, D4-RANGE, D6, I7, MF1, MF6, MF15, PL14, I13a, I13b) using deterministic verification methods (regex, hash, schema validation). Produce lint_report with automated_checks results. Then run subjective assessment for remaining items (C1-C7, I1-I6, I8-I12, D1, D3, D5, D7-D10). Merge results: test_report.automated_results = lint_report, test_report.subjective_results = { items: [subjective_item_ids], average_score: weighted_average }, test_report.confidence_split = '{automated_count}/{total_count} items automated'.
ThreeDimTest.step6: MULTI-FILE AGGREGATE (Pattern B+): Grade each file independently. Composite score = average of per-file weighted scores (using tier-appropriate weights per STANDARD.dynamic_weights). Additionally check MF1-MF38 per STANDARD.multi_file_guidelines: MF1 (version consistency), MF2 (delegation target match), MF3 (shared parameter consistency), MF4 (cross-file data contracts — FORMAT/NAME/VALUE/PATH), MF5 (data_file fallback), MF6 (AIAP.md sync + node count verification + NODE COUNT PRECISION CHECK (CR2 v1.2.0): for each module in AIAP.md modules[], parse the corresponding .aisop.json mermaid field to count actual Mermaid nodes (all '[NodeName]' and '([NodeName])' patterns including Start and endNode). Compare with AIAP.md declared 'nodes: N'. If mismatch: difference ≤ 3 → YELLOW 'MF6-NODE-COUNT: AIAP.md declares {module} nodes={declared} but Mermaid graph has {actual} nodes. Node list: [{node_names}]'; difference > 3 → RED 'MF6-NODE-COUNT-CRITICAL: {module} node count severely mismatched — declared {declared}, actual {actual}. AIAP.md or Mermaid graph may be outdated.' Node counting rules: count all Mermaid node definitions matching regex /\[\w+\]|\(\[\w+\]\)|\(\(\w+\)\)/ — includes decision nodes, process nodes, Start, endNode), MF7 (routing parameter symmetry), MF8 (semantic operation consistency), MF9 (cross-module node invocation — bidirectional), MF10 (system_prompt consistency), MF11 (Error strategy consistency), MF12 (tools union verification), MF13 (persisted data schema versioning), MF14 (cross-module field consumer validation — BASE: verify each cross-module parameter has matching producer and consumer declarations; EVOLUTION-FLOW (B5 v1.18.0): for Evolve mode, additionally scan each LEVEL_B change's step text for cross-module data flow declarations using pattern extraction: 'pass {field} to {module}' → producer=current, consumer=target; 'receive {field} from {module}' → producer=source, consumer=current; 'delegate to {module} — pass {field}' → producer=current, consumer=target; 'load {field} from {source}' → consumer=current. For each extracted flow: (a) PRODUCER CHECK — verify producer module functions contain output/return referencing field, (b) CONSUMER CHECK — verify consumer module functions reference field in steps, (c) PARAMETER CHECK — if field passes through parameters, verify consumer declares it, (d) TYPE CHECK — if both ends declare types, verify compatibility. Report: data_flow_validation: { flows_checked: number, flows_valid: number, flows_broken: [{ producer, consumer, field, issue }], flows_unverifiable: [{ producer, consumer, field, reason }] }. flows_broken → YELLOW. All valid → PASS. SKIP for Create mode or when no LEVEL_B cross-module changes exist. MULTI-HOP CHAIN VALIDATION (B1 v1.18.0): after single-hop flow validation, build directed data flow graph from all extracted flows. Detect chains with 3+ hops (A→B→C or longer) using transitive closure. For each multi-hop chain: (a) INTERMEDIARY CHECK — verify each intermediary module explicitly documents BOTH receiving data FROM upstream AND forwarding it TO downstream. If intermediary lacks explicit forwarding specification: flag MF14-INTERMEDIARY-GAP with chain path and missing direction. (b) FIELD NAME CHAIN CONSISTENCY — validate parameter/field names match at each hop in the chain. If hop N uses 'growth_signals' but hop N+1 uses 'growth_data': flag MF14-CHAIN-NAME-MISMATCH with suggestion to unify. (c) CHAIN REPORT — output: { chains_checked, chains_valid, intermediary_gaps: [{ chain_path, intermediary_module, missing }], name_mismatches: [{ chain_path, hop, expected_field, actual_field }] }. Any gap or mismatch → YELLOW), MF15 (output contract enforcement — validate module outputs against declared json_schema at pipeline stage boundaries; N/A for contracts without json_schema), MF16 (tool directory consistency — verify tool_dirs references, mcp_server.json exposed_tools match, agent_card hash consistency; Pattern G only, N/A for others), MF17 (mandatory output completeness — verify AIAP.md, quality_baseline.json, agent_card.json exist in target directory; BLOCK if missing per STANDARD), MF18 (mechanism conflict scan per STANDARD.core MF18 — within each function, identify steps that modify the same output target e.g. response_text or final_output; if 3+ steps modify same output, verify mutual exclusion controls exist: explicit exclusion clauses like 'if step X already fired SKIP' or 'max 1 per turn', frequency controls like cooldown or session quota, conditional gating like mood check; no mutual exclusion found → YELLOW 'MF18-MECHANISM-CONFLICT: steps [{step_ids}] in {function} all modify {output_target} without mutual exclusion'; generate conflict matrix: step × step → {compatible, exclusive, conditional}; EVOLVE MODE: when evolution adds new steps targeting existing output, check against ALL existing steps targeting that output; SKIP for functions with ≤2 output-modifying steps), MF19 (data lifecycle completeness audit per STANDARD.ecosystem MF19 — for Pattern B+ programs with 2+ modules sharing data entities: trace storage locations across all modules, build data residency map { entity_name → [module.file locations] }; verify deletion operations cover ALL storage locations — if entity stored in 2+ modules but deletion covers only subset: YELLOW 'MF19-ORPHAN-RESIDUE: entity {name} deletion incomplete in [{uncovered}]'; verify export operations include all distributed copies; if program declares GDPR compliance in safety module, escalate orphan residue to RED; SKIP for single-module programs or Create mode), MF20 (cross-module signal consumption audit — verify emitted signals have consuming steps in target modules; orphan signals → RED, name mismatches → YELLOW; BYPASS PATH DETECTION (CR4 v1.2.0): for each module with cross-module calls detected, parse Mermaid graph to enumerate ALL paths from Start to endNode. For each path, check whether it includes a cross-module call step (patterns: 'Call {program}.{module}', 'delegate to {module}', 'pass to {module}'). If some paths include cross-module calls but other paths to endNode do NOT, generate advisory: YELLOW 'MF20-BYPASS-PATH: Module {A} calls {B} in path [{P1_nodes}] but not in path [{P2_nodes}]. Verify if path {P2} should also notify module {B}. Common cause: block/unblock or error paths that skip integration steps.' This is advisory-level (YELLOW) because not all paths require the same cross-module calls — some paths legitimately bypass certain modules. Include in MF20 report: bypass_paths: [{ module, signal_target, paths_with_call: [path_ids], paths_without_call: [path_ids] }]), MF21 (intra-function step mutual exclusion audit — verify 3+ steps modifying same output have shared gate variable; consumes MF18 conflict_matrix; no gate → YELLOW), MF22 (safety gate signal enforcement audit — verify safety module gate/throttle signals are checked by downstream modules; unenforced safety signals → RED non-waivable), MF23 (name-version embedded consistency), MF24 (protocol document consistency — scan evolution changes for new entities without protocol coverage), MF25 (tool integration completeness — verify all tool touchpoints updated when tools[] changed), MF26 (enum registry consistency — cross-module enum value coverage audit), MF27 (new module dependency symmetry — compare new module dependencies against existing patterns). MF34 AIAP_TOOLS VERSION STALENESS CHECK (B2 v1.7.0 — OWASP Dependency-Check SCA transitive dependency detection; Snyk 2025 reachability analysis and staleness scoring; GitLab 2025 dependency scanning CI-integrated; SCA freshness score propagation): For Pattern G programs (programs with tool_dirs declarations in AIAP.md), verify that nested sub-programs in AIAP_Tools/ directories are not critically outdated compared to their counterparts in the workspace. PURPOSE: detect version staleness in nested tool dependencies — discovered empirically when aisop_auto_evolution contained Creator v1.24.0 in AIAP_Tools while workspace Creator had evolved to v1.6.0. METHOD: (1) EXTRACT TOOL_DIRS: from target AIAP.md, read tool_dirs[] entries. For each entry with type='aiap_program', extract path and resolve to absolute directory. (2) READ SUB-PROGRAM VERSION: for each resolved AIAP_Tools sub-directory, read AIAP.md to extract sub-program name and version. If AIAP.md missing → YELLOW 'MF34-NO-METADATA: AIAP_Tools/{path} lacks AIAP.md — cannot verify version.' (3) WORKSPACE SCAN: scan workspace_dir for directories matching sub-program name pattern ({name}_aiap/ or aisop_{name}_aiap/). If found, read workspace copy's AIAP.md version. (4) VERSION COMPARISON WITH SEMVER FULL PARSE (B4 v1.8.0 — SemVer 2.0.0 specification semver.org; OWASP Dependency-Track VersionDistance policy major/minor/bugfix gap + age policy; Masterminds/semver Go library pre-release edge cases): parse both versions using full SemVer specification: PARSE FORMAT: major.minor.patch[-prerelease][+buildmetadata]. PARSE RULES: (a) Extract major, minor, patch as integers. Leading zeros in major/minor/patch → YELLOW 'MF34-PARSE: version {v} has leading zeros — invalid per SemVer §2'. (b) Pre-release: if '-' present after patch, extract pre-release identifiers separated by '.'. Per SemVer §9: pre-release version has LOWER precedence than associated normal version (1.7.0-beta < 1.7.0). (c) Build metadata: if '+' present, extract but IGNORE for comparison (per SemVer §10: build metadata MUST be ignored when determining version precedence). (d) Invalid format (not matching major.minor.patch pattern) → YELLOW 'MF34-PARSE: version {v} does not follow SemVer specification — treating as unknown version.' COMPARISON: for same major, compute version_gap = workspace_minor - tools_minor. If tools version has pre-release but workspace doesn't (same major.minor.patch) → treat as 1 additional minor gap (tools is unstable, workspace is stable). If both have pre-release identifiers → compare per SemVer §11 (numeric identifiers compared as integers, alphanumeric compared lexically). Compute version_gap = workspace_minor - tools_minor (for same major). If version_gap > 2 minor versions → YELLOW 'MF34-STALENESS: AIAP_Tools/{path} has {sub_name} v{tools_ver} but workspace has v{workspace_ver} ({gap} minor versions behind). Consider updating nested tool.' If version_gap > 5 minor versions → RED 'MF34-STALENESS-CRITICAL: AIAP_Tools/{path} has {sub_name} v{tools_ver}, workspace has v{workspace_ver} ({gap} versions behind). Nested tool may lack critical fixes and features.' If major version differs → RED 'MF34-MAJOR-MISMATCH: AIAP_Tools/{path} has {sub_name} v{tools_ver} but workspace has v{workspace_ver}. Major version difference indicates incompatible API.' (5) NO WORKSPACE MATCH: if sub-program not found in workspace → INFO 'MF34-NO-REFERENCE: {sub_name} not found in workspace — cannot verify staleness.' (no score impact). (6) REPORT: mf34_staleness = { tools_scanned: int, tools_current: int, tools_stale: [{ path, sub_name, tools_version, workspace_version, gap, severity }], tools_unknown: int }. SKIP: for non-Pattern-G programs (no tool_dirs), or when workspace_dir not available. MF35 FLOW_FORMAT DECLARATION CONSISTENCY (v1.14.0): verify AIAP.md flow_format field matches actual program files in the target directory. METHOD: (1) READ flow_format from AIAP.md frontmatter. If missing → YELLOW 'MF35-MISSING: AIAP.md lacks flow_format field (required per §3.1 v1.14.0+). Infer from files.' (2) SCAN directory for .aisop.json and .aisip.json files. Determine actual_format: only .aisop.json → 'AISOP', only .aisip.json → 'AISIP'. Note: in DUAL mode each directory only contains one format's files, so actual_format is always singular per directory. (3) COMPARE: if flow_format != actual_format → YELLOW 'MF35-MISMATCH: AIAP.md declares flow_format={declared} but directory contains {actual_format} files. Update flow_format to match.' (4) SEED DOMAIN CROSS-CHECK: if flow_format='AISOP' verify seed domain contains 'aisop'; if flow_format='AISIP' verify seed domain contains 'aisip'. Mismatch → YELLOW 'MF35-SEED: flow_format={format} but seed={seed} — consider aligning seed domain.' Report: mf35_flow_format = { declared: string|null, actual: string, match: bool, seed_aligned: bool }. MF36 NAME-VERSION DRIFT DETECTION (B2 v1.15.0): scan all .aisop.json files' name fields for embedded version patterns. METHOD: (1) For each module's system.content.name field, apply regex /v?(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)/ to extract embedded version. (2) If version found: compare with system.content.version field. If mismatch → YELLOW 'MF36-NAME-DRIFT: {module} name contains v{embedded} but version is {actual}. Auto-fix candidate.' Mark auto_fix_candidate=true for ReviewStep NAME_FIELD_SYNC. (3) Also scan system.content.description for version patterns — if description references a specific version (e.g., 'v1.14.0:') and that version != current version field, flag: YELLOW 'MF36-DESC-STALE: {module} description references v{stale} but current version is {actual}.' (4) MF36_COMPREHENSIVE_FIELD_SCAN (B2 v1.17.0 — SonarQube multi-rule field scanning; ESLint cross-file version consistency): extend drift detection beyond name and description to additional metadata fields. ADDITIONAL FIELDS: (a) system.content.summary: scan for embedded version patterns /v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+/. If version found AND != current version → YELLOW 'MF36-SUMMARY-STALE: {module} summary references v{stale} but current version is {actual}. Auto-fix candidate.' (b) functions.*.constraints: for each function's constraints field, scan for version patterns in context of version-specific references (e.g., 'per v1.14.0 spec', 'added in v1.15.0'). If version references found AND version < (current_version - 2 minor): → INFO 'MF36-CONSTRAINT-HISTORIC: {module}.{function}.constraints references v{old} — verify if reference is historical documentation (acceptable) or stale requirement (needs update).' NOTE: constraint version references are often intentionally historical (citing when a rule was introduced), so severity is INFO not YELLOW. (c) SKIP: system_prompt field (natural language, version references are rare and contextual). (5) Report: mf36_name_version = { modules_scanned: int, drifts_found: [{ module, field, embedded_version, actual_version }], fields_scanned: ['name', 'description', 'summary', 'constraints'] }. SKIP for Create mode (version is always fresh). MF37 SUMMARY_LENGTH_COMPLIANCE (B3 v1.2.0 — AIAP Protocol §3.1 recommended ≤500 characters; JSON Schema maxLength validation; OpenAPI 3.1 description best practices): verify AIAP.md summary field length is within protocol limits. METHOD: (1) Read AIAP.md summary field via file_system. (2) Measure character count. (3) SCORING: len > 500 → RED 'MF37-SUMMARY-OVERFLOW: AIAP.md summary is {len} chars, protocol §3.1 recommends ≤500. Must truncate — remove changelog entries, keep concise capability overview only.' len 400-500 → YELLOW 'MF37-SUMMARY-APPROACHING: AIAP.md summary is {len} chars, approaching 500 limit.' len ≤ 400 → PASS. (4) ALSO CHECK each module .aisop.json summary field: len > 500 → YELLOW 'MF37-MODULE-SUMMARY-LONG: {module} summary is {len} chars.' (5) Report: mf37_summary_length = { aiap_md_summary_len: int, aiap_md_status: PASS|YELLOW|RED, module_summaries: [{ module, len, status }] }. RATIONALE: summary is a discovery-facing metadata field consumed by T1 trust operations and A2A AgentCard — changelog entries belong in evolution_notes, not summary. MF38 (AGENT_CARD_CONSISTENCY (B3 v1.2.0 — A2A Agent Card spec validation a2a-protocol.org; JSON Schema multi-file consistency ajv.js.org): verify agent_card.json is internally consistent and consistent with AIAP.md + .aisop.json modules. CHECKS: (a) VERSION MATCH: agent_card.version must equal AIAP.md version field. Mismatch → YELLOW 'MF38-VERSION: agent_card.json version ({ac_version}) does not match AIAP.md ({aiap_version})'. (b) SKILL COUNT MATCH: count of agent_card.skills[] must equal main.aisop.json NLU intent count. Mismatch → YELLOW 'MF38-SKILLS: agent_card has {ac_count} skills, main has {main_count} intents'. (c) MODULE COUNT MATCH: count of AIAP.md modules[] must match actual .aisop.json file count (glob *.aisop.json, exclude AIAP_Standard files). Mismatch → YELLOW 'MF38-MODULES: AIAP.md declares {declared} modules, found {actual} .aisop.json files'. (d) DESCRIPTION METRICS: extract numeric claims from agent_card.description (node count, scenario count, MF range). Cross-validate against quality_baseline.json and AIAP.md. Stale metrics → YELLOW 'MF38-METRICS: agent_card.description claims {metric}={old_value}, actual is {new_value}'. (e) SAFETY CARD PRESENCE: verify agent_card.json contains safety_card with risk_level, data_handling, limitations. Missing → RED 'MF38-SAFETY: agent_card.json missing safety_card'. Score: 0 issues → 5, 1-2 YELLOW → 4, 3+ YELLOW or RED → 3). MF7-AISIP CONTROL FLOW EXISTENCE (B1 v1.10.0): when target_format == 'AISIP': PASS if aisip.main is dict AND len(keys) >= 2 AND at least one node value == {} (end node exists). FAIL if aisip.main is empty or missing. When target_format == 'AISOP': existing check unchanged (contains 'graph' or 'flowchart'). When target_format == 'DUAL': PASS if BOTH format checks pass. MF31-AISIP CONTROL FLOW-FUNCTION CONSISTENCY (B1 v1.10.0): when target_format == 'AISIP' or 'DUAL': extract flow_nodes = set(aisip.main.keys()). Compare with function_nodes = set(functions.keys()). missing_functions = flow_nodes - function_nodes → RED 'AISIP nodes without function definitions: {missing}'. orphan_functions = function_nodes - flow_nodes → YELLOW 'unreferenced function definitions: {orphans}'. When target_format == 'AISOP': existing Mermaid extraction unchanged. When target_format == 'DUAL': run BOTH checks, report discrepancies between formats. Each MF violation counts as 1 issue in Intrinsic dimension of the main file's grade. SCORE TRAJECTORY ANALYSIS (B5 v1.2.0): For Evolve mode (when .evolution_snapshot/quality_baseline.json exists): (1) Load snapshot baseline scores (C, I, D, weighted). (2) After computing current scores, calculate per-dimension delta: delta_C = current_C - snapshot_C, delta_I = current_I - snapshot_I, delta_D = current_D - snapshot_D, delta_weighted = current_weighted - snapshot_weighted. (3) For each dimension with negative delta (regression), identify the specific check items that scored lower than snapshot and annotate with root cause: 'Score Impact: {dimension} {delta:+0.000} — caused by {check_id}: {description}'. (4) Include in test_report: score_trajectory = { snapshot_version, snapshot_scores: {C, I, D, weighted}, current_scores: {C, I, D, weighted}, deltas: {C, I, D, weighted}, regression_causes: [{ dimension, check_id, old_score, new_score, cause }], improvement_causes: [{ dimension, check_id, old_score, new_score, cause }] }. (5) Pass score_trajectory to ReviewStep for display in PresentDraft. SKIP for Create mode (no snapshot baseline).
ThreeDimTest.step6_5: AISIP STRUCTURAL CHECKS (B1 v1.10.0 — Phase 2 corrected phased evolution plan; doc15 §5.2 Phase2-B1 AISIP-CHECKS): CONDITION: execute ONLY when target_format == 'AISIP' or target_format == 'DUAL'. SKIP when target_format == 'AISOP' (all existing checks sufficient). PURPOSE: verify AISIP structural integrity using type-free inference rules from AIAP_Standard.runtime_extensions.aisop.json. These checks complement C5/MF7/MF31 dual-format enhancements by adding AISIP-specific validation. CHECKS: (1) AISIP-C1 ENTRY NODE SANITY: first_key = list(aisip.main.keys())[0]. IF aisip.main[first_key] == {} → RED 'AISIP-C1: first node "{first_key}" is end — program terminates immediately. Entry node must have outgoing transitions.' (2) AISIP-C2' STRUCTURAL LEGALITY (replaces VALID_TYPES check per E6 correction): FOR each node_name, node in aisip.main.items(): IF node == {}: continue (end node, valid). allowed_keys = {'next', 'branches', 'error', 'wait_for', 'delegate_to'}. unknown = set(node.keys()) - allowed_keys. IF unknown → YELLOW 'AISIP-C2: node "{node_name}" has unknown flow-layer keys: {unknown}. Only {allowed_keys} are valid in flow layer.' RESERVED_KEYS MISPLACEMENT CHECK: reserved_in_flow = set(node.keys()) & {'on_error', 'retry_policy', 'context_filter', 'output_mapping', 'map', 'join', 'constraints'}. IF reserved_in_flow → RED 'AISIP-C2: node "{node_name}" has RESERVED_KEYS in flow layer: {reserved_in_flow} — these belong in functions layer per AIAP_Standard.runtime_extensions.' (3) AISIP-D1 END NODE EXISTS: has_end = any(n == {} for n in aisip.main.values()). IF not has_end → RED 'AISIP-D1: no end node found — flow never terminates. At least one node must be {} (empty dict).' (4) AISIP-D2 BRANCH REFERENCE INTEGRITY: FOR each node_name, node in aisip.main.items(): IF 'branches' in node: FOR branch_label, target in node['branches'].items(): IF target not in aisip.main → RED 'AISIP-D2: branch target "{target}" (from "{node_name}".branches."{branch_label}") not found in flow.' (5) AISIP-D3 NEXT REFERENCE INTEGRITY: FOR each node_name, node in aisip.main.items(): IF 'next' in node: FOR target in node['next']: IF target not in aisip.main → RED 'AISIP-D3: next target "{target}" (from "{node_name}") not found in flow.' (6) AISIP-D4 JOIN WAIT_FOR INTEGRITY: FOR each node_name, node in aisip.main.items(): IF 'wait_for' in node: FOR waited in node['wait_for']: IF waited not in aisip.main → RED 'AISIP-D4: wait_for target "{waited}" (from "{node_name}") not found in flow.' (7) AISIP-I1 DELEGATE TARGET INTEGRITY: FOR each node_name, node in aisip.main.items(): IF 'delegate_to' in node: sub_name = node['delegate_to']. IF sub_name not in aisip (top-level keys, i.e., sub-task flow definitions) → YELLOW 'AISIP-I1: delegate target "{sub_name}" (from "{node_name}") not defined as sub-task flow. May reference external program.' REPORT: aisip_checks = { format: target_format, checks_run: int, checks_passed: int, issues: [{ check_id: 'AISIP-{X}', node: string, severity: 'RED'|'YELLOW', message: string }] }. Include in test_report. AISIP check issues contribute to Intrinsic dimension scoring: each RED → -0.5, each YELLOW → -0.2 (capped at -2.0 total). DUAL MODE: run AISIP checks on the .aisip.json files; AISOP checks on the .aisop.json files (existing logic). Report both separately. DUAL-SPECIFIC CHECKS (B1 v1.11.0 — Phase 3 corrected phased evolution plan §6.2; simplified DUAL per E3 correction): Execute ONLY when target_format == 'DUAL'. (8) DUAL-C1 FUNCTIONS IDENTITY: extract aisop_functions from .aisop.json and aisip_functions from .aisip.json. Perform deep recursive comparison of all keys and values. diff_items = deep_diff(aisop_functions, aisip_functions). IF diff_items is empty → GREEN 'DUAL-C1: functions 100% identical across AISOP and AISIP versions.' IF diff_items is non-empty → RED 'DUAL-C1: functions differ between formats. Differences: {diff_items}. Expected: functions are byte-identical (only §4 topology differs).' NOTE: no need for extract() separation — functions structure is identical across protocols per corrected phased evolution plan E3. (9) DUAL-D1 NODE COUNT MATCH: aisop_count = count Mermaid nodes from .aisop.json. aisip_count = len(aisip.main.keys()) from .aisip.json. IF aisop_count == aisip_count → GREEN 'DUAL-D1: node count consistent ({aisop_count}).' IF aisop_count != aisip_count → YELLOW 'DUAL-D1: node count mismatch — AISOP has {aisop_count} nodes, AISIP has {aisip_count} nodes. Investigate topology translation accuracy.' (10) DUAL-D2 TOPOLOGY ISOMORPHISM (simplified per E3): FOR each node_name in aisop_nodes: extract aisop_edges from Mermaid graph (outgoing edges from node_name). extract aisip_edges from aisip.main[node_name] (next[], branches values, error, delegate_to targets). Compare edge sets: aisop_targets = set(aisop_edges). aisip_targets = set(aisip_edges). IF aisop_targets != aisip_targets → YELLOW 'DUAL-D2: topology divergence at node "{node_name}". AISOP edges: {aisop_targets}. AISIP edges: {aisip_targets}.' IF all nodes match → GREEN 'DUAL-D2: topology isomorphic — Mermaid and JSON flow describe identical graph structure.' NOTE: only pure topology is compared — no cross-layer mapping needed because functions are shared. DUAL SCORING: when target_format == 'DUAL', ThreeDimTest runs separately on AISOP files and AISIP files. final_score = min(score_aisop, score_aisip). This ensures the weaker format determines the overall grade. Report: { score_aisop, score_aisip, final_score, dual_checks: { DUAL_C1, DUAL_D1, DUAL_D2 } }. DUAL check issues contribute to Detail dimension: each RED → -0.5, each YELLOW → -0.2.
ThreeDimTest.constraints: TIER-ADJUSTED SEVERITY: Simple -> I2 is WARNING (at least type guard required), I3 skip if no file_system. Standard/Professional -> I2 is FAIL (all 5 guards), I3 is FAIL with file_system. MULTI-FILE: MF violations are Intrinsic-severity. Report both per-file grades and composite score. COMPLEXITY ADVISORY: step3 (I1-I13 intrinsic checks) and step4 (D1-D10 detail checks) each contain 10+ evaluation items in a single step. This density mirrors the STANDARD checklist structure — splitting would fragment the evaluation context needed for cross-check correlation (e.g., I2 findings inform I8 scoring). DECOMPOSITION PLAN: If either dimension exceeds 15 check items, split into sub-steps: step3a (I1-I7 core intrinsic), step3b (I8-I13 security+ecosystem intrinsic). Current state: 13+10 items, at threshold but justified by evaluation context dependency.
ThreeDimTest.Error: Unparseable -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: partial grading -> inform.

TrafficClassify.step1: GREEN — auto-apply silently: Error field addition, input validation addition, boundary rule text, minor formatting.
TrafficClassify.step2: YELLOW — auto-apply + report: restructuring suggestions, metabolic cleaning, missing constraint additions.
TrafficClassify.step3: RED — pause for human: fractal restructuring, parameter space changes, flow redesign, grade drop >=2 levels. CROSS-FILE GRANULARITY: version/id-only changes across files -> GREEN. Same-module internal changes (e.g. generate + its helper) -> YELLOW. Cross-module data contract changes (e.g. research output format affects generate input) -> RED. AIAP-TOOLS CLASSIFICATION: trust_level violation in aiap_tools/ -> RED. governance_hash mismatch in aiap_tools/ -> RED. circular dependency detected -> RED. permissions excess in aiap_tools/ -> RED. sub-program missing optional fields -> YELLOW. recursion depth approaching limit (depth=2) -> YELLOW.
TrafficClassify.step4: Generate traffic_report: { green:[...], yellow:[...], red:[...], dim_grades:{C,I,D} }.
TrafficClassify.constraints: Cross-file classification per CROSS-FILE GRANULARITY rules in step3. Score impact >=1.0 -> RED.
TrafficClassify.Error: Classification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: default all items YELLOW -> inform.

PostGenCheck.step1: Check traffic_report for unresolved RED items. If present -> PauseHuman.
PostGenCheck.step2: Display summary: 'Green N | Yellow N | Red N | C:{g} I:{g} D:{g} | weighted:{score}'.
PostGenCheck.constraints: RED items only gate. Weighted score threshold is checked by main.aisop.json QualityGate after generation completes.
PostGenCheck.Error: Gate check fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat as RED present, route to PauseHuman -> inform.

PauseHuman.step1: Present each RED item: what/why/proposed-change/before-after. SUB_MERMAID DECOMPOSITION DISPLAY: when RED item has severity='RED_WITH_FIX' and contains sub_mermaid_proposal, present enhanced view: (a) PATTERN — show detected decomposition pattern (FAN_OUT/SEQUENTIAL/CONDITIONAL) with explanation, (b) BEFORE — show current Mermaid graph with node count and highlight nodes to be extracted, (c) AFTER MAIN — show proposed main Mermaid with SubRef delegation nodes replacing extracted clusters, (d) AFTER SUB — show each proposed sub-graph Mermaid with Entry routing and handler nodes, (e) NODE COUNT COMPARISON — table showing before (N nodes) vs after (main: M nodes + sub1: K1 nodes + sub2: K2 nodes), (f) FUNCTION MIGRATION — list which functions move from main to sub-graph, (g) RISK ASSESSMENT — note any cross-references that may break.
PauseHuman.step2: Wait: approve, reject (continue with warning), or modify. For sub_mermaid_proposal: [Apply] executes decomposition via ApplyFixes.step1_5, [Reject] keeps current structure with fractal_exempt advisory, [Modify] allows user to adjust split points before applying.
PauseHuman.constraints: Never auto-approve RED items. Sub_mermaid decomposition proposals MUST show full before/after diff preview before user decision.
PauseHuman.Error: Timeout -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat as rejected, SaveDraft with warnings -> inform.

ApplyFixes.step1: Apply GREEN silently. Apply YELLOW and log each.
ApplyFixes.step1_5: SUB_MERMAID DECOMPOSITION APPLY (B8 fix execution): When traffic_report contains RED_WITH_FIX item with fix_type='sub_mermaid_decomposition' AND human approved via PauseHuman: (1) BACKUP current aisop object (deep copy in-memory). (2) REPLACE main Mermaid — set aisop.mermaid to sub_mermaid_proposal.main_mermaid. (3) ADD SUB MERMAID — for each entry in sub_mermaid_proposal.sub_mermaids, create aisop.<name> object with { mermaid: entry.mermaid, functions: { Entry: entry.entry_function, ...entry.handler_functions } }. (4) ADD DELEGATE FUNCTIONS — for each delegate in sub_mermaid_proposal.delegate_functions, add to aisop.functions (the main graph's functions). (5) SET FRACTAL_EXEMPT — if sub_mermaid_proposal.fractal_exempt is non-null, add fractal_exempt field to the sub-graph's content object. (6) MIGRATE FUNCTIONS — move extracted handler functions from main functions{} to sub-graph functions{}. Remove from main, add to sub. Preserve all step/constraint/Error content exactly. (7) VERIFY — recount nodes in main and each sub-graph. Assert all <= 15. If verification fails, ROLLBACK to backup and report as RED unresolved. (8) LOG in generation_report: { sub_mermaid_applied: true, pattern: <pattern>, main_nodes_before: N, main_nodes_after: M, sub_graphs_created: [{ name, node_count }] }. POST-SPLIT NODE VERIFICATION STRENGTHENED (CR6): After any sub_mermaid decomposition execution, MANDATORY verification: (1) Count functional nodes in main_graph (Mermaid nodes minus Start, endNode). Verify count <= 16. (2) Count functional nodes in EACH sub_graph. Verify each count <= 16. (3) If ANY graph exceeds 16 nodes post-split: RED 'Post-split violation: {graph_name} has {N} nodes (> C5 threshold 16). Decomposition must be revised.' Abort decomposition and restore backup. (4) Record fractal_exempt documentation in module aisop metadata: 'fractal_exempt': '{total} nodes across {sub_count} sub_mermaid graphs ({graph_name1} {count1}, {graph_name2} {count2}), all under C5 threshold'. (5) RECURSIVE CHECK: if any sub_graph itself has a sub-delegate node, recursively verify that nested sub-graphs also satisfy C5 threshold.
ApplyFixes.step2: FIX ORDER: isolated -> local per-function -> cross-function one-at-a-time. MULTI-FILE ORDER: sub-modules first, main last.
ApplyFixes.step3: VERSION RULES per STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management: modify_only→patch+1, evolve_level_c_only→patch+1, evolve_level_b→minor+1, evolve_level_a→major+1, mixed→highest applicable level wins. NAME FIELD VERSION SYNC (CR9): When updating the 'version' field in .aisop.json files during evolution: (1) For each file, read the 'name' field. (2) If name contains a version pattern (matching regex /v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+/), replace the embedded version with the new version. Example: name='soulbot_chat v1.8.0' with new version '1.10.0' → name='soulbot_chat v1.10.0'. (3) If name does not contain a version pattern, leave name unchanged. (4) Record name_version_synced: true in pipeline_metadata for each synced file. This prevents MF23 violations from recurring after every evolution.
ApplyFixes.step4: DESCRIPTION HYGIENE: if description contains more than 2 version history entries (e.g. 'v1.1.0 adds... v1.2.0 adds... v1.3.0 adds...'), compress into summary: keep current version changes in full, collapse older entries into single line 'Prior versions added: [comma-separated feature list].' SUMMARY CONSISTENCY: after description update, verify summary field still reflects current capabilities. If evolution added key features (new metrics, new modules, new tools) that summary doesn't mention, update summary. If evolution removed features that summary still mentions, update summary. Summary should remain 1-2 sentences — do not add version history. DERIVED REFERENCE AUDIT: full-file scan for internal consistency — (a) NAME VERSION SYNC: if name field contains embedded vX.Y.Z, update to match version field. Apply to ALL files in multi-file packages. (b) ERROR TEXT CONSISTENCY: scan all Error/fallback fields for hardcoded counts. Cross-reference each against actual list lengths in same function's steps. Fix stale counts. (c) MENU COUNT SYNC: Start/General menu item counts → match actual NLU intent/feature count. (d) EXPLAIN FEATURE SYNC: Explain.step1 feature list count → match Explain.Error referenced count. (e) CROSS-FUNCTION REFERENCE CHECK: if function A references function B by name in steps/constraints, verify B exists in the same file's functions. Auto-fix any mismatches found. Log all fixes in generation_report: { derived_fixes: [{location, old_value, new_value}] }. SMART DESCRIPTION UPDATE (B3 v1.18.0): After DESCRIPTION HYGIENE and DERIVED REFERENCE AUDIT, perform automated capability-aware description update: (1) EXTRACT CURRENT CAPABILITIES: parse existing description field to identify capability phrases — scan for comma-separated feature lists, parenthetical version tags '(B{N} v{X.Y.Z})', and '+' separated additions. Build capabilities_current = [{ phrase, version_tag_if_any }]. (2) COMPUTE CAPABILITY DELTA: for each accepted LEVEL_B change, create capability_addition = { feature_name: change.title condensed to noun phrase, version_tag: '(B{N} v{new_version})' }. For changes that REPLACE existing functionality (same function + same step), mark old capability as superseded. (3) GENERATE UPDATED DESCRIPTION: retain non-superseded capability descriptions, replace superseded entries with new versions, append new additions, update version references. Verify summary remains <= 500 characters (D8-SUMMARY). (4) GENERATE UPDATED MODULE DESCRIPTIONS (AIAP.md): for each modified module, extract new LEVEL_B features targeting that module, update AIAP.md module description line by replacing old version-tagged features with new ones while preserving base role description. (5) SAFETY GATE: if auto-updated description differs from original by >50% character change ratio, classify as YELLOW for human review instead of auto-apply. Log: 'SMART DESCRIPTION UPDATE: {N} capabilities extracted, {M} additions, {K} superseded, description delta {pct}%'.
ApplyFixes.step5: CUSTOM FIELD SEMANTIC CHECK: scan for fields like verified_on, author, tags. Preserve all. For verified_on specifically: if content changed beyond cosmetic fixes, append '(not re-verified after vX.Y.Z evolution)' to description as advisory. Do NOT modify the verified_on array itself.
ApplyFixes.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: in-memory backup. ATOMIC: .tmp rename. Max 3 fix cycles.
ApplyFixes.Error: Apply fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display patched JSON inline -> inform.

SaveDraft.step1: DIRECTORY RULES: Modify -> save in-place at {workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. Evolve -> save in-place (overwrite with .bak backup). Create -> create new directory {workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. DUAL MODE DIRECTORY RULES (B1 v1.12.0): When pipeline_context.target_language='DUAL': (a) AISOP files (.aisop.json) → {aisop_workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. (b) AISIP files (.aisip.json) → {aisip_workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. (c) Create DUAL: create {name}_aiap/ in BOTH aisop_workspace_dir and aisip_workspace_dir. (d) Evolve DUAL: save-in-place per format — .aisop.json in aisop_workspace_dir, .aisip.json in aisip_workspace_dir. DUAL_AIAP_MD_SPLIT_SPEC (B5 v1.14.0): In DUAL mode, the following files differ between directories: { flow_format: 'AISOP' in aisop dir / 'AISIP' in aisip dir, seed: 'aisop.dev' in aisop dir / 'aisip.dev' in aisip dir, governance_hash: computed from local *.aisop.json or *.aisip.json respectively }. All other AIAP.md fields are IDENTICAL between copies. quality_baseline.json and agent_card.json are written identically to BOTH directories.
SaveDraft.step2: Save {file}.aisop.json (or .aisip.json per DUAL rules). Atomic write: .tmp then rename. Backup existing as .v{old_version}.bak (e.g. main.aisop.json.v1.0.0.bak). AIAP.md CONTENT PREPARATION: if AIAP.md content was generated (Create mode) or updated (Evolve mode), prepare AIAP.md content but do NOT write — pass prepared content to ReviewStep for single-ownership write (per main.aisop.json ReviewStep.step3). MULTI-FILE: save each changed .aisop.json file with individual .bak. Unchanged files are NOT rewritten. INCREMENTAL: output content_hash for each written file in draft metadata for downstream stage-skip decisions. WRITE LOGGING: log write results in pipeline context: { files_written: [string], backups_created: [string], atomic_success: boolean, bytes_written: number }. If in pipeline context, append write_log to pipeline_metadata for ObservabilityStep telemetry. MODULE DESCRIPTION TEMPLATE (C3 v1.18.0): When preparing AIAP.md content (Create or Evolve mode), ensure each module description row follows the standard template format: '| **{filename}** | {role} — {base_capabilities}, **{version_features}** | {tools} |'. Template rules: (a) role = module's one-sentence role definition (stable across versions, derived from module summary first sentence), (b) base_capabilities = comma-separated core capabilities (grows with each evolution, old version-specific features demoted here), (c) version_features = bold-marked features added in current version (replaced each evolution), (d) tools = module's declared tools list. For Evolve mode: parse existing AIAP.md module table, identify role/base/version segments per row, demote previous version_features to base_capabilities (remove bold), insert new LEVEL_B features as bold version_features. For Create mode: generate initial template from module summary and description.
SaveDraft.step3: Output: { draft_path, test_report, traffic_report, pattern, version, weighted_score }.

SaveDraft.Error: Write fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display JSON in code block -> inform.

ReflectionCheck.step2: Generate reflection_notes for ReviewFlow. Residuals -> note for next cycle.
ReflectionCheck.Error: Verification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip advisory, set notes = empty -> inform.
